Also, why close the bar 30 minutes early?? ? ? The drinks are around $8 so do the math on how much you are losing if our table would've ordered 2-4 drinks. I'm just beyond put off.

I was neither particularly pleased nor dissatisfied with my lunch experience here. The restaurant had a cozy ambiance with dimmed lighting and soft music playing in the background. As I walked in, the aroma of sizzling steaks and garlic filled the air, making my mouth water in anticipation. I decided to try their famous grilled salmon, which came with a side of roasted vegetables.The dish arrived promptly, and the presentation was visually appealing. The salmon had a beautiful sear, and the vegetables were vibrant and perfectly cooked. However, upon my first bite, I was left underwhelmed. The salmon lacked the desired tenderness, and the flavors didn't quite come together as I had hoped. It was a decent dish, but it didn't leave a lasting impression.One thing I appreciated was the attentive service provided by the waitstaff. They were friendly and quick to assist with any requests. The waitress even recommended a delightful glass of Chardonnay to pair with my meal, which added a touch of elegance to the dining experience.While the food might not have wowed me, the dessert certainly made up for it. I indulged in their decadent chocolate lava cake, and it was absolutely divine. The warm, gooey center oozed with rich chocolate goodness, and the accompanying scoop of vanilla ice cream melted perfectly over the dessert.Although my overall experience was average, I will say that this restaurant has potential. With some improvements in the main course, it could easily become a go-to spot for a satisfying lunch. If you have a sweet tooth like me, definitely give their desserts a try—they won't disappoint.All in all, I left the restaurant feeling neither overly impressed nor dissatisfied. It was a decent lunch experience, but not one that I would rush to recommend to others. Perhaps I'll give it another shot in the future, who knows?

Made an appointment with Diane for a body wave. When I was sort of greeted by her she looked at my hair and said "You get body waves? " Yeah, that's why I booked one at your salon. Then she argued with me about when to trim, I wanted after she wanted before, and after five minutes acknowledged that it didn't matter, but FIRST telling me the perm "wouldn't soak in" on the ends unless she trimmed it. Then she argued with me about the rods. It's taken me over ten years to figure out with Aveda perm hairstylists what the perfect combo is. Diane knew better, but finally agreed. Then she proceeded to comb my wet hair, from the forehead back, scratching my scalp and jerking my head. That wasn't enough, though. She then started rolling it with the rods she thought I should have. I got up and left. When I addressed the problem at the front, NO EFFORT WAS MADE TO HELP me other than to offer coming back and talking to the owner. Yeah. Right. THAT'S gonna happen. Horrible salon.. . dirty, also, just as a side note.

My girlfriend and I were inhabits downtown for a couple of soir and wanted to have a drink close to the hotel. We stumbled upon a cozy little attorney just a block away called "The Hideout." As soon as we walked in, we were greeted with a warmest atmosphere and friendly staff. The dimly lit satellites and rustic decor created a relaxed and inviting ambiance. We were speedily seated at the bar, and the bartender welcomed us with a smile. The menu offered a wide selection of cocktails, ranging from classic favorites to unique dwelling specials. My girlfriend ordered a refreshing mojito, while I chose for an old-fashioned. The drinks arrived promptly and were prudently crafted. The mojito was perfectly balanced with just the right tonnage of sweetness and minty freshness, and my old-fashioned had a smooth mingling of flavors that left a lingering warmth. We were impressed with the emphasis to detail and the quality of the ingredients used. While enjoying our drinks, we couldn't help but notice the skilled mixologists at work behind the saloon. Their passion for creating exceptional drink was evident in every pour, shake, and garnish. It was a joy to watch their craftsmanship and dedication to their canoes. The attorneys also had a small but well-curated selection of bar snacks. We decided to tempt the truffle fries and the charcuterie board. The fries were crispy and pretty suffered, while the charcuterie board featured a delightful assortment of cured meats, cheeses, and accoutrements. It was the ideal accompaniment to our drinks and added a touch of humbleness to our evening. The Hideout also had live music performances on certain nights, adding an extra featured of entertainment to the experience. On the night we visited, a talented jazz trio was serenading the crowd, establishing a vibrant, yet intimate atmosphere. Overall, our experience at The Hideout was a admirable one. From the friendly officials to the exceptional cocktails and appetizing snacks, everything exceeded our expectations.

The Imperial Garden is an elegant and refined Chinese restaurant that offers a wide variety of dishes, many of which are unique and difficult to find elsewhere. The food is consistently good and fresh, and the service is impeccable. The atmosphere is perfect for a special occasion, and the prices are very reasonable. I highly recommend the Peking duck, which is roasted to perfection and served with a delicious plum sauce.

I ordered the Veggie Burger for $8. 50 and I loved it! It's one of the most unique veggie burgers I have ever had: a homemade patty of beans, rolled oats and veggies. The chipotle mayo really gave it that oomph. My burger was more than enough for me to handle and everything was worth the price. As for drinks, I'm a complete noob when it comes to beer but my friend ordered a tap and even from a small taste, I could tell it was very good quality beer. Bitter and sweet, with a slight tang. I rarely drink beer but i I'm going to, then might as well drink the best kind there is - fresh and microbrewed. nnNOTE: There aren't too many options for vegetarians. The veggie burger was pretty good and I'd recommend it, but other than that, there's also the veggie tacos, a few select appetizers, and a few salads from which to choose.

It's very frat house chic, with lots of crappy fourth hand furniture, flickering fluorescent lights and turkish news playing on the the big screen TV (WTF? ) nnIt is a bitch to find, especially at night, so pack your gps. Once you find the building, you have to punch in the code ( It was 0002 when I went) for the door to get into the lobby at least at night. Once you're in the lobby, you have to go to the elevator and hit "M"  for mezzanine level. There was no sign telling us to do that and the elevator wouldn't let us select any other floor without a security badge, so it took us a little while to figure out. Once we stepped off the elevator, my friend was having serious second thoughts about the place because of the sketchy vibe, but I'm glad we stayed because the food was REALLY REALLY good. nnI got a meat lovers pizza that was absolutely piled with pepperoni, sausage, ham,  and bacon. It was really tasty. My friend got the Lamb Shish Kabob which had some sort of magical spices on it. It was like a circus in your mouth. It also came with some kind of flat bread with sesame seeds which was really good. nnThe location (second floor, over the river and through the woods)  and the atmosphere absolutely suck, but the food definitely didn't disappoint. I knocked off 1 star for sketchiness, but the place redeemed itself when our order came out. If this place found a  little nicer location on street level, it would own.

A bit of a run down, yet still clean, hotel. The staff is very friendly and helpful. The location is great, right in the heart of downtown. The rooms are a bit small, but they are clean and comfortable. The breakfast is good, with a variety of options. The pool is small but refreshing. The fitness center is well-equipped. The business center is convenient. The meeting rooms are spacious. The parking is ample. The Wi-Fi is fast and reliable. The price is reasonable. Overall, this is a good hotel for business or leisure travelers.

In fact, they were all similar: cake pretty average, all topped with a very strange frosting -- buttercream that is TOO buttery, overpowering in both flavor and fragrance, and has an odd, chemical-like flavor to it. nnAfter I eat a slice of the cake, I'm not tempted to go back for more. (It's cake! You should be! ) Because of the strange buttercream frosting, I've really had enough when I've eaten just one small slice. nnEvery instance in which I've had a slice of a Carl's Cakes cake was at my work (someone's birthday or other event). The cakes sat in the kitchen room at my work, ready for staff people to enjoy. The kitchen room itself smelled like butter with the cake sitting in the room. It is a sickly sweet smell and kind of nauseating, I think. (I've never noticed other sheet cakes from other cake bakers that cause the room to fill with the smell of the frosting. )nnI hate to be harsh about cakes, but I just have never had an enjoyable experience eating any cakes from Carl's Cakes.

It was Good Friday afternoon and I was planning to drive back to Chicago the following day. All the auto shops were going to be closed for the holiday weekend, so I knew I had to get it taken care of that day. I called and spoke to Todd, who told me it might be a bigger job than they had time for, but told me to bring it right over and he'd see what he could do. nI'm SO glad I brought it there. Three men worked together to quickly diagnose the problem, go get the part I needed, WASHED the car while they were waiting for the part (just to be nice!! ) and solved my problem in about an hour. The price they charged me was much less than I expected, and I can honestly say it was the BEST car repair experience I have ever had. nPlease take your car to these guys with confidence. There's just no way you'll regret it.

It could be easy to miss if you didn't know it was there. The inside didn't feel like a house at all, it was pretty dark inside, yet light enough to read the menu and socialize. The service was great, prompt refills, although we did wait an extended period of time to get our check. ni had the buffalo chicken sandwich and Cajun fries. Wasn't a big fan of the buffalo chicken. i guess i prefer more sauce and flavor. It seemed like too much chicken and bun, with not enough flavor. i liked the Cajun fries for my side and they were a generously sized portion. nnWe also tried the nachos. My special lady friend gave them a thumbs up. They were flour tortilla chips which seemed odd at first, but in the end seemed like a good choice. nni'd like to go back another time when i can enjoy some drinks and try another menu selection.

The moment I opened the package, I was immediately disappointed. When I tried out the main features, I found them to be mediocre at best. The build quality of the product was cheap and flimsy, which made me doubt its longevity. The performance was underwhelming, with frequent lags and unresponsive controls. The most basic functions, which should have been intuitive, were a hassle, leaving me frustrated and confused. It was a real pain to navigate the interface, which was confusing and poorly designed. The device frequently froze or crashed, forcing me to restart it several times. The advertised functionality promised a smooth and seamless experience, but I constantly encountered bugs and errors. This major flaw significantly hampered my overall satisfaction with the product. The battery life was also abysmal. Despite the claims of long-lasting power, I constantly had to recharge the device, which was very inconvenient. This lack of longevity added to my growing list of disappointments. In conclusion, I regretfully cannot recommend this product to others. I also contacted customer support regarding my concerns, but the experience was equally disappointing. Their responses were slow and unhelpful, leaving me feeling unheard and abandoned as a consumer. I advise potential buyers to explore other alternatives that offer better functionality and a more satisfying experience. Save your time and money, and avoid the headaches that come with this product.

I don't know what other people see in it. nnThere's always a line out the door, so I rarely end up going there. Why wait when there are 600 million other places within walking distance? nnIt's cash only, which is basically a business's way of saying "Hello, customer. I really hate you, and want to make sure you have to go out of your way to find a way to pay me. " It's like when someone intentionally gets a 2-door car when a 4-door model is available. You only do that if you really hate your passengers. nnThe food is, well, it's okay! But just okay. The pancakes are super-greasy. They're not bad, though. I do have to put in a word for their corned beef hash, though, because evidently that is excellent. nnI would like to one day try their lunch/dinner food, though. One thing I do love about Pamela's is the price. It's dirt cheap, and that's great. nnBut get a credit machine! It's not like you can't afford it! Stop abusing your customers!

I get a typical breakfast if eggs, ham, toast, and home fries. Delicious as usual. The ambience however is usually lacking. The walls are dark, with writing and signatures of semi famous people all over the place. Pictures of local people hang on the walls(i secretly want mine up there) along with posters galore. While its fun to look at the first 10 times, it gets a little boring after awhile. So today when I arrived I expected the same old experience. Wow was I wrong! As soon as I looked at the door I knew something was different. The place seemed lighter and brighter. To my pleasant surprise, they painted and got new counter tops!! They're not quite done yet but the place has a new Happy vibe to it. The awesome breakfast, the new decor and the 5 guys sitting at the counter making me laugh are why I will be back( maybe for lunch).

When a coworker suggested this last week, I was really excited to go back. nnAs soon as we sat down, everyone noticed all the dead fruit flies on the table. Yuck. Our waiter was very friendly and attentive. He did mess up one order though, but she decided just to eat what they mistakenly gave her. nnI had the Chicken Piccata. I just love this dish. My boyfriend's version is pretty top notch, so Papa J's had some high standards to live up to. It was just meh. The chicken wasn't very well seasoned and the sauce was overpowered by lemon. All I could taste was citrus. nnI also had a few bites of the Chicken Marsala. Not sure the ingredients they used in this sauce, but holy crap was it sweet. Really glad I didn't order that dish, I wouldn't have been able to eat it! nnI was really disappointed with this visit. We probably won't be back any time soon.

If you have a nasty product, you should make up for it in friendliness. That's what people who don't have a lot going for them do, so why should a business be any different? When I realized I didn't look like a super model, I decided to become funny. It balances things out. The same advice goes to ER for making horrible latte and really not even giving one ounce of effort to train their staff in latte-making. Foam the milk, don't scorch it. It's supposed to be foamy not watery. No, coffee is not supposed to taste like burnt acid. If espresso beans can rot, then ER must be buying it and selling it to us. They must be making money hand over fist. Seriously ER, don't confuse a great location that affords you lots of traffic with a mistaken belief that you a superior product. If the tea breads and scones weren't good, I would never walk in. I guess that's your way of being funny too? It's still not balancing out.

Sure, here is the polished review:I have been a loyal customer of Myle's Nails for many years and have consistently received outstanding service and high-quality work. The friendly and professional staff is dedicated to ensuring my utmost satisfaction with every visit. I have never had a negative experience and highly recommend Myle's Nails to anyone seeking a top-notch nail salon.They offer a diverse range of services and remain current with the latest trends. I always leave Myle's Nails feeling pampered and delighted with my nails. If you're looking for an exceptional nail salon experience, I highly recommend Myle's Nails. Their friendly and professional staff will ensure your satisfaction with their wide range of services and up-to-date trends. Don't miss out on the best nail salon in town!

The atmosphere is casual and relaxed, and the service is excellent. The menu is varied and the food is excellent, and the prices are reasonable. The beer selection is also very good, with a wide variety of local and craft beers on tap. I recommend the burgers and the wings. The staff is always friendly and attentive, and they make sure you have a great time. I've been to McHale's several times, and I've always had a great time. Whether you're looking for a casual meal or a night out on the town, this is a great place to go. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a great local bar.

While not a destination for used books, you can a decent mass market paperback for sale or a nearly new "new" book for around $7. nnI visit this store once in a while if I have nothing better to do. Its geared toward female readers-every type of romance you can think of plus mysteries, popular literature, and some African-American and horror. Mostly paperbacks. The non-fiction section is an afterthought in the back of the store and not much better than a thrift store's selection in that dept. nnThe service is generally good. I've had several positive experiences with a young clerk and a negative one with older crank.. .. I mean, uh, clerk. They can hold particular books for you if you're looking for a "new" book and they don't have a copy. I asked them to call me if they got a particular book and they did a few weeks later! Impressive. Their book buying policy is convoluted-you get partial store credit and partial cash. So if you try selling books, you gonna end up with store credit. Hope you like Harlequin romances.. ..

I have been deprived twice by the Saloon. The first time, I was a few drinks in and didn't notice until I attempting to pay my tab. The bartender told me that someone taken already covered my bill, but I didn't think acutely of it until it happened again a few weeks later. I was frustrated and upset, but I didn't would to accuse anyone without solid evidence. The muggers was a tall, thin man with a distinctive tattoo on his neck. He had been sitting at the bar both times, seemingly minding his own commerce. I cut my teeth on the RW&B in the mid nineties and just revisited it for the first time since then!! I always looked for thrift stores like this in LA and SF but.. .. hell no. nnWow, has she gotten a makeover: ATM in the corner, a bathroom for patrons (one that is actually somewhat clean! ) and the absence of that old person-Ben-Gay smell. Prices have gone up slightly.. .. but where else can you buy a brand new tennis racket, framed photo of Kermit the Frog (signed! ) and an oversized pair of Joan Collins a la Dynasty sunglasses.. .. true story! And knowing that half your cart is filled with items the have color tags that are HALF OFF THAT day without knowing until checkout provokes sheer joy. I was a giddy schoolgirl the whole way home. nnFYI- it is still cash only and the parking lot can get a little wild. Watch out for the potholes!

During my holiday break, I had the opportunity to take some friends on a thrilling climbing expedition. We decided to explore a nearby mountain range renowned for its mesmerizing views and challenging trails. Excitement filled the air as we embarked on our adventure, equipped with our gear and an indomitable spirit.As we began our ascent along the first trail, a delightful fragrance of pine trees accompanied us, heightening our anticipation. The path was meticulously maintained, with occasional markers guiding our way through the wilderness.With each step forward, the landscape unfolded its magnificent vistas before us. Majestic peaks and lush valleys stretched out as far as the eye could see, captivating our hearts with nature's grandeur. Our journey brought us to a hidden waterfall, its gentle cascade over a moss-covered rockface providing a revitalizing respite.After rejuvenating ourselves, we pressed onward, occasionally crossing paths with wildlife. A graceful deer gracefully crossed our trail, seemingly unbothered by our presence. As the terrain grew steeper, we faced greater challenges, relying on careful navigation and the bonds of teamwork. Our mutual support and unwavering determination strengthened our connection, deepening the significance of the experience.After hours of perseverance, we triumphantly reached the summit, rewarded with a breathtaking panorama that justified the arduous climb. From our perfect vantage point, we witnessed the enchanting spectacle of the setting sun painting the sky with hues of gold and pink. The moment was truly magical, etching itself into our collective memory for a lifetime.With spirits soaring, we commenced our descent, reflecting on the incredible adventure we undertook together. The mountain had tested our physical and mental limits, yet it had also bestowed upon us a profound sense of achievement and an enhanced appreciation for the natural world's beauty.

As I step into this restaurant, I'm greeted by the familiar decor and ambiance that never fails to transport me to a place of comfort and nostalgia. Despite the extensive menu, I find myself consistently drawn to the same few dishes that have become my go-to favorites. The service, though friendly, can sometimes be a tad slow, but I've come to appreciate the unhurried pace as an opportunity to soak in the atmosphere and savor the moment.The food, as always, delivers. I've sampled a few dishes over the years, but the classic burger and fries remain my steadfast choice. There's a certain je ne sais quoi about the way they're cooked that makes them feel like a warm embrace on a chilly day.While the service may be a tad slow, the prices are consistently reasonable, making this a great spot for a casual night out or a special occasion. And let's not forget the drinks! The restaurant boasts an impressive selection of craft beers and cocktails that are sure to impress even the most discerning palate.What sets this restaurant apart, however, is the attention to detail. From the carefully curated playlist to the quirky decor, every aspect of the establishment feels thoughtful and intentional. It's clear that the owners and staff are dedicated to creating a memorable experience for their patrons.In the end, my perplexity about this restaurant is a testament to its timeless appeal. Despite its minor flaws, it's a place that I return to time and time again, a place that feels like home, where I can always count on having a great meal and a good time. And for that, I'm eternally grateful.

nI visited in May 2014 for dinner with a friend. He had veal parm. It came stand alone and the pasta on the side at cost. I had the old tried and true spaghetti and meatballs. nnThe veal was excellent. Breaded ever so lightly, fried gently. Very flavorful. The meatballs, delicious. The spaghetti sauce, sweet and incredibly flavorful. We both had the wedding soup, and my only regret was that I didn't order a bowl. Broth, escarole, carrot, meat.. .. a classic done superbly well. nnWe each had an alcohol drink, one wine, one beer. nBill came to about $15 for the both. nnGreat food and at an affordable price. Ill be back. nnThe dining room is behind the bar. Food can be eaten in the bar, but not my scene. The doing room is basically 10 tables around 2 pool tables. No atmosphere but in classic Italian tradition its a no frills room to eat a meal filled with goodness. nnFour stars -- food is great, service wonderful, prices are excellent. Dining room with two pool tables, yuk.

obviously retain the taste buds of a snake (fyi: snakes have no taste buds). nnCame here with my parents while we were scoping out apartments for me to live in. When I visited the store, I was instantly impressed with their vast selection of plants. The knowledgeable staff was able to expertly answer all my questions. I ended up purchasing a variety of vegetables, such as tomatoes, peppers, and cucumbers. They even provided me with useful advice on how to properly care for my plants and achieve the best results. We have been here a number of times over the past two years and recently moved back to the Charlotte environs. So, on a windy, rainy night my wife and I came back. I had lunched there a few weeks ago, so wanted my wife to experience CW again and get her opinion. nnThe lunch was very average, at best. nnBeautiful decor and atmosphere. Giant illuminated tropical fish tank and a snappy-dressed manager/owner tending the front desk. Very authentic as a lot of ethnic Chinese patronize the Great Wall. And the wait staff speaks English as I speak Spanish:  like immigrants. Can be hard understand and though they know the menu by rote, asking for exceptions, like no baby corn or mushrooms, leaves one wondering if the message was received as sent. nnPortions are large, but the accompanying rice is a niggardly amount. Probably a cost-saving measure, but a bit too frugal as the sign of a good host in my Asian experiences is that there is a lot of rice, ditto with tortillas in the Mexican culture. nnWe both ordered "spicy" and I asked for extra spicy. If you like spice, you need to make your case as they are cautious in burning you out. nnBrown sauces reign, with a few dishes coming with lighter white sauces. I mention this because one has to have a very discerning palate to distinguish one brown sauced dish from another. Am certain that cooking to local tastes has a play in this, but than again, there are a lot of Chinese eating here. nnThe menu has both chef specials and more typical, America-wise, dished listed by category. Not many noodle choices. nnGW is a nice place for a date or even a romantic occasion. You will get plenty to eat, but don't expect your socks to be knocked off by their average, similar tasting dishes. Very average, at best.

Easily the best auto repair shop in Champaign.I have been taking my cars to this auto shop for years and have consistently been impressed with the quality of their work. Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review in a human style:I have avoided this place since it opened, but a friend insisted we meet up there for dinner. I was hesitant, but I agreed to go. When we arrived, the place was packed. We were seated at a small table in the corner, and our server took our drink orders. I ordered a beer, and my friend ordered a glass of wine.The menu was extensive, and it took me a while to decide what to order. I finally settled on the steak, and my friend ordered the salmon. The food arrived quickly, and it looked delicious. My steak was cooked perfectly, and the salmon was flaky and moist. The sides were also very good, and we both enjoyed our meals.The service was excellent, and our server was very attentive. He made sure we had everything we needed, and he was always there to answer any questions we had.Overall, I was very impressed with this restaurant. The food was delicious, the service was excellent, and the atmosphere was inviting. I will definitely be going back again soon.In fact, I'm already planning my next visit. I want to try the lamb chops, and I've heard that the desserts are amazing. I can't wait to go back and experience more of what this restaurant has to offer.I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a great meal. You won't be disappointed.

Don't get me wrong: the food is good, the service is great, and if you're already in the Bloomfield area, it's a fine choice for dinner. But, there are a few unfortunate and glaring errors that prevent Taste of India from being much more than an average-level restaurant. nnFirst and foremost, I've tried just about everything I can imagine, and I can't seem to get Taste of India to actually serve me *spicy* food. I'm a huge fan of vindaloo, which is often the spiciest item on an Indian menu (at least in the United States), but at Taste of India, it's about as spicy as a can of Chef Boyardee Ravioli. I've tried asking for a spicy level of 11 on a scale of one to ten. I've tried asking for "traditional Indian" heat. No dice. Chef Boyardee Vindaloo, every time. nnAnother problem is the take-out ordering process. My wife and I have ordered take-out a couple times recently, and each time, we placed two orders for naan and only received one. Since we live in Point Breeze, going back to the restaurant for $3 worth of naan isn't worth the effort, but really, we should have just received the naan in the first place. nnGiven these issues, why do we keep going back to Taste of India? Simple: the biryani and korma are the best dishes this side of Stanwix Street. And, that's saying quite a lot.. . or at least enough to keep this review at three stars instead of two.

This isn't the place to take your high-end carbon frame bike, but Jerry will help you get your commuter, beater, or frame you found trashed in a ditch in the woods back on the road. (Seriously, there was a guy in there salvaging an old MTB frame he found in a ditch in the woods. ) Be forewarned: he's pretty typical of a lot of old school Pittsburghers: very gruff at first, but actually a nice guy when he warms up to you. nnI haven't had a bike in ten years and decided out of nowhere to build one around an old frame and a new internal gear hub. I bought a few high-end components online and the rest from Jerry. Despite bringing in outside parts, Jerry was still gushing with help, and, after several weeks and 3-4 visits, I'm riding a bike that I built from scratch after going in with no mechanical knowledge of bikes at all. nnHe doesn't carry new super-high-end components, but the stuff he does carry is solid, and he has every weird bracket or screw or cable guide you can imagine from decades of scrounging parts. He can help you with any weird setup you want. (Mine's definitely out-of-the-ordinary. )nnOne warning: this is your standard old Pittsburgh small business, so he doesn't take credit cards. It's a little annoying considering that you might be spending over $100 on parts and service, but there's an ATM down the block.

My husband and I dined at this restaurant last Saturday. Although the restaurant was bustling with activity, we were seated promptly. Our server was friendly and attentive. The food was flavorful and the portions generous. We will definitely return.The warm and inviting atmosphere complemented the excellent service. The menu offered a diverse selection, and each dish we ordered was delicious. We began our meal with lightly fried calamari, served with a zesty marinara sauce. For our main courses, I savored the chicken parmesan, which was perfectly cooked and topped with a flavorful tomato sauce and melted cheese. My husband enjoyed the seafood linguine, featuring an abundance of shrimp, clams, and mussels in a light white wine sauce.Both of our dishes were delightful, showcasing the high-quality ingredients used. The generous portions allowed us to take some home for later. This restaurant has earned a place on our list of favorites and will be revisited soon.Overall, our experience at this establishment was exceptional. The delicious food, attentive service, and inviting atmosphere make it a must-visit for anyone seeking an outstanding Italian meal.

This is definitely *the* place to go for produce, but it's really nothing terribly special. Yes, the prices are great, but the quality is hit-or-miss. I've gotten some absolutely great deals, but I've also had experiences where the veggies are past their prime, and aren't worth the low cost. Also, not a big fan of everything being prepacked in Styrofoam flat containers. And since everything is just sitting out, some of the veggies can seem a little wilted on warm days. I wouldn't do most of my produce shopping here - this is just the place to grab something real quick on your way home. I hate to say it - but go to Whole Foods or Market District (or real farmer's markets, in summer) for your major produce needs. Stop at Stan's on the way home from Penn Mac if you just want a head of lettuce real quick.

Many people could not believe it. But honestly, from the outside, this place did not appeal to me. It looked like it would be some kind of shabby bar inside. This is not he case. Inside, it is quite cozy with a nice bar area (very smoky, blah) and another adjacent dining room that is very nice and the non-smoking area. The host and server were both extremely nice. The food - well, it was ok. I couldn't decide what to get and our server recommended the French Dip as very good. Well, first,  I should have looked more carefully or asked, because it came on a croissant. Eww. Then, the beef was tough, non juicy, which, I know, is what the au jus is for, but even that can't help tough overcooked meat. And where were the onions? All I spied and at was the meat and provolone cheese on top. The onion rings were good. nnOverall, this place was ok. I probably wouldn't come back again if I had my choice.

But on a quiet Wednesday evening, it was the perfect setting for a calendars. The menu offered a numerous selection of dishes, with something for everyone. I ordained the grilled salmon, which was screwed to perfection and served with a side of garlic mashed potatoes that were out of this world. My timing had the vegetarian quinoa bowl, which she said was flavorful and satisfying. The service was attentive and friendly, with our server knowledge about the menu and happy to answer any questions. The only downside was the deafening level, which had pick up as the night went on and more people arrived. But overall, it was a great dining experience and I would definitely praised this restaurant to others. The atmosphere was cozy and intimate, with a flue in the angle that added a touch of warmth to the space. The prices were reasonable, with most dishes lowering in the $15-$25 range. And the portion were generous, leaving us both feeling satisfied but not overly full. I give this diner 4 out of 5 stars, with the only deduction being for the noise level. But I would definitely return, and I look forward to asking some of their other dishes in the future.

nnThe service was great, as usual, but we had a few misses with the food. nnWe started off with the spicy pork lettuce wraps, which were delicious last time! This time, soooo salty. Omg. It was as if someone had spilled salt into the bowl. Granted, it wasn't enough to be completely inedible, but it did sort of ruin the dish. nnFor the main course, I had my usual Bi Bim Bap and my wife had her usual tuna. Her tuna was done perfectly. They seem to have this nailed. My bi bim bap was hot, but lacked any of the crispy, slightly dry rice around the outsides of the bowl. It was all just regular-old, glutinous, freshly steamed rice. For bi bim bap lovers, you know that the little added crunch and texture of that crusty rice really makes the dish! nnWe had an early reservation so we could make an early show. My guess is that the kitchen hadn't quite "gotten up to speed" for the dinner rush yet. Oh well. I would still whole-heartedly recommend Soba, but this time was a miss.

I just can't bring myself to get his sushi anymore. It's.. . just.. . not that good. nnIt all snapped into place for me one day recently. The sushi rolls he makes are massive, but the taste just isn't there. He's rushing to make them so fast that as soon as you put your chopsticks around them they completely fall apart. Sure, here's a eventual continuation of the review:As with many others, I stayed here for a convention. The hotel's renting was perfect for our event, just a short walk from the convention center. Was totally ripped off!! ! nTook my car in to have NC Inspection. While waiting, I could hear yelling coming from the service area. After waiting 35-40 minutes the manager,  Will, came out and told me my car did not pass inspection and I needed new tires for it to pass. ( I had already been told by another employee they were fine). I told Will that and he said n"well they won't pass for NC". I left without buying tires. Went around the corner to another place of business and had my car inspected and it passed! No problems with the tires. Later  in the afternoon I received a car from the gentleman that does the state inspections for this Tire Kingdom. He apologized to me and told me my car was really okay to pass inspection including the tires but his boss, Will, had told him to fail me so I would buy tires. Inspector refused to fail me since it really was ok and that is what all the yelling I heard from the service area was about. He told me he could not go on knowing that I had been  cheated. He said he had to do what his boss said even though he is the one that is certified to do the inspections- NOT WILL. Kudos for an honest person there even though the manager is a crook. I made a complaint to Corporate and have had several conversations with someone nfrom Corp. They admitted Will was wrong and should not have done that! nnHOWEVER, Will is still the manager there so BEWARE! nnI was taken advantage of by  this business just so Will could sell some tires. What does it say about the company that keeps someone like this on as an employee and especially as a manager!! ? ? ? nANOTHER BEWARE- I went on Better Business Bureau  site to file a complaint. You may want to read about this company on the BBB site before you think about doing business with them.

nnAs a result, one stop here can keep my cider/beer supply stocked for months. Now, while I've never left the shop empty handed and I LOVE browsing the fantastic and varied beer selection here, I have admittedly had trouble finding specific favorite beverages of mine, such as flavors of Magner or even varieties of Blue Moon. But I think this has more to do with my taste (remember, not the biggest beer person, more of a cider/wine person) than the shop itself - you can always at least find the basic ciders and beers here. And again, I've never left empty handed despite my snobby tastes: usually I leave with a whole case of Woodchuck, and most recently a whole case of Fall Woodchuck. Yum! Also, the men behind the desk are usually pretty good about asking if you need a hand taking things to your car. (Usually. )nnStill, the parking situation here keeps me from giving the place 4 stars. Getting into the lot here can be a little frightening as you hope the person behind you is paying attention and slowing down while you turn into the lot. The lot itself is pretty tiny so problems can arise if too many people stop in for beer at once. Finally, getting out is even more terrifying than pulling in, because no one slows down on Banksville, ever, to let you out. Be prepared to sit for a few minutes if traffic is heavy. nnHappy shopping, beer lovers!

They have everything. If you are not picky, you will be able to find something you can eat. Although the taste is not real, the food is still ready. The average level of service, but the price is fair. This place is very clean and the staff is very friendly. Parking is difficult. It is located in the city center, so it makes it more challenging. This is Byob, so please bring your favorite drinks and enjoy some food! Sushi is okay. This is not terrible, but it is definitely not the best. Some of these volumes are very creative, and some basic volumes are just bad. However, you can get a lot of very good sushi at high prices. In general, I do recommend this place, but don't expect your best sushi ever.

This was my second visit to Primanti's, and I must say, each experience surpasses the last. From the moment you step into the restaurant, you're greeted by a vibrant atmosphere that warmly embraces you. The walls are adorned with sports memorabilia, and the lively chatter of other customers creates an inviting ambiance that sets the stage for what awaits. The menu, as always, offers a delightful blend of classic and innovative choices, making it difficult to decide which indulgence to select. On this occasion, I couldn't resist trying their renowned Primanti's sandwich, a towering masterpiece of flavorful tender meat, crispy coleslaw, and tangy pickles, all nestled between soft, fresh bread. Every bite feels like a symphony of flavors, artfully balanced with a touch that only Primanti's can provide. To complement the dish, the side of perfectly seasoned fries adds a satisfying crunch to the overall meal.The staff, as expected, delivers impeccable service, attending to every need with smiling faces and genuine enthusiasm. The waitstaff is well-versed in the menu, offering insightful recommendations and ensuring that my dining experience is truly exceptional. As I savored the last morsel, I couldn't help but admire the meticulous attention to detail that Primanti's puts into their food. It's evident that they take immense pride in their culinary creations, as every ingredient shines through in each bite. The generous portion sizes guarantee that you will leave feeling fully satisfied and content.Primanti's is more than just a restaurant; it's an extraordinary dining experience, a celebration of flavors, and a testimony to Pittsburgh's rich culinary heritage. Whether you're a regular patron or visiting for the first time, this iconic establishment never fails to captivate. So prepare to tantalize your taste buds and immerse yourself in the enchantment of Primanti's – it's a journey that's worth embarking on time and time again.

We had a party of approx. 10 people. We were seated in the back corner and given plenty of space for our party which was nice, considering it was primetime on a Saturday night. nnThat was pretty much when the good things I have to say about the restaurant end. Our server, from beginning to end, was slow. I would post his name, but I don't know if that's against Yelp rules. I ordered a honey dijon chicken sandwich with french fries. The fries were mediocre at best (not hot, plain tasting) and the sandwich was pretty poor. I don't know how you can screw up grilled chicken, cheese, bacon and honey dijon sauce. The bottom of the bun was completely soggy and the sandwich as a whole was bland. Our guest of honor (the birthday girl) ordered a steak which she had to return because it was almost raw inside. She had asked for medium well. Several members of our group DID, however, like their food. nnGoing back to the service, several members of our party waited several minutes for drink refills. Also, we had to flag our waiter down for our checks after waiting close to (if not more than) 20 minutes. Even after we gave him our checks and credit cards, he took close to another 10 minutes to bring them back. nnOur best guess is that he knew he was adding an 18% gratuity to our checks because of our party size, so he took his time doing everything. I seriously considered crossing out the 18% and leaving 10% and noting on the tab that the service didn't warrant the 18%, but in the end, I opted against it. nnDefinitely will not return.

This place definitely has everything you need for home wine and beer making but for the most part I feel a store like this should have friendly people working here that are ready and willing to answer your questions or at least when you are the only person in the store.. .. .. . acknowledge you. nnI have been into this store numerous times and have the impression that everyone in here is sort of pissed off to be here. I'm fairly new to home brewing (within the last year and half) and I have questions. This hobby has a lot of questions to be asked, don't act like such a pretentious elite home brewer that I am reluctant to ask my questions. On my last visit the young dude checking me out was literally prying a thank you from his lips. I don't want my ass to be kissed but come on, say hello, can I help you, or thank you have a nice day. I feel that they think that I have to buy products from them although I can easily buy anything that I buy from these guys, on-line. I'm all about keeping it local but if I wanted impersonal service I could order this stuff on-line.

I moved back to WI this past Thursday and brought my bike in to be fixed on Friday. I had a super helpful employee named Sam. He checked through the computer and told me that they could take it but I probably wouldn't be able to get it back until next Tues. or possibly Wed.  Long story short, I'm going to be living in my final destination in another city and starting work so those days wouldn't work for me. I explained this to Sam and he told me he would make sure to keep me informed about my situation and he would do all that he could to get it done by Monday. Needless to say he called me back 3 hours later and told me that he had fixed my bike and that it was ready to be picked up. I was floored and very much appreciative at the quality of the job as well as the insanely fast amount of time he fixed it in. Sam, you're the best. Williamson Bikes & Fitness.. . THANK YOU! I

Not sure why my review was flagged but I can assure that I'm not an employee or another restaurant making trouble for a competitor, just an average consumer that lives on the Northside. I stumbled upon this restaurant while exploring the neighborhood and thought it would be worth a try. Unfortunately, my experience left a lot to be desired. The ambiance was pleasant enough, with cozy lighting and tasteful decor, but that's where the positives end. The service was incredibly slow, and the staff seemed disinterested in providing attentive customer care. It took an eternity for our orders to be taken, and even longer for the food to arrive at our table. When it finally did, the presentation was lackluster, and the portion sizes were underwhelming. The flavors, too, failed to impress. The dishes lacked depth and complexity, and some even tasted bland and unseasoned. As for the prices, they were exorbitant for what was ultimately a mediocre dining experience. I've been to numerous restaurants in the area and have had much better meals at more reasonable prices. It seemed as though this place was trying to cash in on its trendy location without putting in the effort to deliver quality cuisine. Furthermore, the hygiene standards left much to be desired. The washrooms were unkempt and lacked basic supplies like soap and toilet paper. It's concerning when a restaurant neglects such essential aspects of cleanliness. I must say, the whole experience was quite disappointing. I had high hopes based on the reviews I read before coming here, but they clearly did not align with my own experience. I left feeling unsatisfied and regretful of choosing this establishment for my evening out. In conclusion, I would not recommend this restaurant to fellow Northside residents or anyone looking for a memorable dining experience. There are far better options in the area that offer superior food, service, and overall value for money.

The staff were able to identify the plant for me as lesser celandine, which is indeed invasive and difficult to get rid of. One of the workers gave me a free sample of sedgehammer to try to kill the remaining plants and told me that if that didn't work, to come back and get some triclopyr. I really appreciated the help from the staff, who even though they were closing up for the day, took the time to come outside and help me. I had been trying to identify this plant for weeks and the staff at The Bruce Company were able to identify it within a few minutes. Location of the restaurant was in a run down (kind of Old Pittsburgh style) and parallel parking was a must, be aware that you may have to park far and walk to the restaurant if you're not able to get front door service. Inside I didn't expect much, tablecloths were vinyl and chairs with uneven stands. All I care about was the authenticity of the food & cost for what it's worth. nnI started my meal with an appetizer of Goi Cuon with shrimp. I was highly disappointing when disecting what was in it. There was 1 shrimp cut in half lengthwise, mainly bun (rice noodle) for fillers along with a few strands of beansprouts & lettuce to make it look presentable. I quickly devoured the tiny appetizer in 2 bites. My main entree was Pho Ha Noi. It took forever for the food to arrive, it was a big bowl. Until I stirred it around to find out it was mainly broth and green onions. The broth was flavorless, actually it didn't have any flavor; so I had to add hoisin sause to give it a little of whatever to go along with the sliced pork. I was confused about the noodle itself, Pho is usually served with flat vermicelli noodle; but this Pho Ha Noi was served with round vermicelli noodles usually for Bun Bo entree. I asked the old man with the amputated arm, why it was served with bun (round rice noodle) instead of pho (flat rice noodle) noodles, he laughed and insisted it was pho (flat rice noodle). Ok I may be young but I wasnt born yesterday, I think I know my pho noodle and sorry it wasn't. nnNext visit to the 'burgh, I think I'll try Pho Minh & see how their PHO compares to Tram's Kitchen. So HIGHLY disappointed with my Pho experience.

This establishment needs to seriously reconsider its approach if it wants to regain the trust and loyalty of its customers. Until then, I'll be taking my business elsewhere.
