Loca𝚝ed in 5th Aveոue Place downtown, Cafe Amante definitely serves its purpose for a sit-down eatery as it's the only true sit-down restaurant in the building. Walk into the cafe, the cozy and inviting atmosphere immediately catches your attention. The warm lighting, comfortable seating, and tasteful decor create a pleasant ambіence for diners. The staff grеets you with a friendly smile and promptly escorts you to a table of your choice. The menu offers a diverse sеlection of dishes, catering to different palates and dietary preferences. Whether you're craving a hearty breakfast, a light lunch, or a satisfying dіnner, Cafe Amante has something for everyone. The food is consistently deli cious, with fresh ingredients and well-balanced flavors evident in each bite. One standout item is their firma pasta dish, which is cooked to perfection and generously portioned. The presentation of the dishes is also striking, as each plate is stylishly arranged, showcasing the chef's attention to detail. The service at Cafe Amante is commendable, with the servers being attentive and kոowledgeable about the menu. They are quick to offer recommendations and acϲommodate special requests. The prices are rеasonable considering the quality of food and service provided. The prtion sizes are generous, ensuring that you leave the restaurant satisfied. The only minor drawback is the limited parking options nearby, which can be a challenge during рeak dining hours. However, this shouldn't deter you from visits Cafe Amante as it is well worth the ѵisit for a delightful dining experience in downtown.

When I arrived at the restaurant, I was greeted by a hostess who seemed confused about the menu and prices. She told me that the website was incorrect and that they had updated their prices. I was hesitant to believe her, so I asked to see the new menu. She brought me a menu that was clearly printed on a home printer, with handwritten notes scribbled all over it. I was not impressed.I ordered the enchilada and wheatgrass, as I had planned, but when I went to pay, the cashier told me that the prices on the menu were not valid. She explained that they had updated their prices, but failed to provide any documentation or evidence to support her claim. I was taken aback by her attitude and lack of professionalism.I asked to speak with the manager, but she refused, saying that he was not available. I left my phone number with her, hoping that he would contact me to resolve the issue.The next day, I received a call from the manager, who was very defensive and dismissive of my concerns. He insisted that their website had been down for four years, and that they had updated their prices to reflect the cost of ingredients. He refused to offer any compensation for the confusion and inconvenience.I was disappointed, but not surprised, given the poor service I had received the previous day. I decided to do some research and found that the restaurant had received several negative reviews in the past year, all citing similar issues with the menu and service.I will not be returning to this restaurant, and I would advise others to avoid it as well. The prices are not clearly stated, the service is poor, and the management is unresponsive to customer concerns. It's a shame, because the food could be delicious, but the overall experience is not worth the risk.

I have not eaten in the dinner area yet. So my opinion is solely based off of the sushi bar area. This place is mediocre at best, the tofu was ok, a little blane. Disappointed in the miso soup, it had this weird taste that I can't quite place. The sushi was pretty good for the most part. Almost everything had cream cheese in it, I do not like cream cheese in my sushi and had to ask for it without. The server took forever at first but was friendly; the sushi chefs were very nice and friendly as well. I probably won't come back to eat but it was ok to try at least once. I just know better sushi places. They had a crappy and small sake selection and their cheapest bottle was 18. I didn't even bother to get anything; I love sake and I always get it. Most of the time I can find something I like but they just had such a little selection of nothing good. Also I had to get a new soy sauce bowl. The one I had was dirty. I feel you should pay more attention to that. This place is ok. I don't know how the dinner part is but after my experience I have low expectations of it.

The broth is rich and savory, the noodles are cooked to perfection. The beef is tender and the herbs are fresh. I also like the fact that they offer a variety of toppings, such as bean sprouts, lime wedges and fresh chili peppers, which allow me to make my pho to my liking. The restaurant is clean and well maintained, and the cozy atmosphere makes me feel at home. The service is also excellent. The staff is friendly and attentive, and they always ask me if I need anything and refill my water glass. I would recommend Pho Van to anyone looking for a delicious and satisfying meal. Although the prices are a little higher than other Vietnamese restaurants in the area, the quality of the food and service more than makes up for it. I'm looking forward to my next visit!

The hostess and bartender were great. They great you with a smile and pleasantly wait on you. The food is just OK. nnThe hostess greated us with a smile and mentioned it would be about 15 minutes. We asked if we could eat at the bar and he said of course. We found a small booth at the bar. On our way back we noticed that there were several empty tables available. We found this strange and that is one reason I gave them 3 stars. nnWe were greated by the bartender, who was quite nice looking, with a smile. He had alot of tables and still seemed to manage pouring drinks and delivering food. He was great at his job. nnI ordered tortilla soup and salad with raspberry vinaigrette and it was good, delivered with a smile, and timely. nnThe bartender kept our drinks full as well. nnI would give the service 5 stars, food 3 stars, size of the restaurant is large but they never seem to be staffed for it so that i would give 1 star. nnGreat place to go for a quick lunch! !

However, if you're looking for a unique dining experience, this restaurant might be worth a visit. Their menu features a mix of traditional and modern dishes, all made with fresh, locally-sourced ingredients. The presentation is also impressive, with each dish carefully plated to create a feast for the eyes as well as the taste buds. The service is attentive and knowledgeable, with the staff happy to answer any questions you may have about the menu or the food. The atmosphere is upscale and intimate, making it a great choice for a special occasion or a romantic evening out. Overall, while it may not be the best value for money, this restaurant offers a high-quality dining experience that is worth the splurge.

I visited this establishment on a weekday afternoon, expecting to be greeted by a friendly staff eager to assist me. However, as soon as I walked through the door, I was met with complete indifference. Not a single person acknowledged my presence, let alone offered to help me find what I needed. I wandered around the store for several minutes, feeling like a ghostly apparition invisible to the human eye.Finally, I spotted an employee standing behind the counter, staring blankly into space. I approached them and asked if they had the item I was looking for. They barely looked at me before shaking their head and telling me they didn't have it. No apology, no offer to check if they had it in stock, nothing. I left the store feeling frustrated and disrespected.This experience was in stark contrast to the excellent service I received at a similar business just a few days earlier. There, I was greeted warmly by multiple staff members, who offered to help me find what I needed and even went out of their way to show me other options. It's clear that this establishment has a lot to learn about customer service.I would not recommend this business to anyone. The lack of customer care and attention is appalling, and it's evident that the staff are not trained to handle basic customer interactions. If you value your time and dignity, avoid this place at all costs.

nnBUT. .. nnThe prices are 10-25% higher (sometimes more) than Woodman's in Sun Prairie, the produce leaves much to be desired in terms of quality and the check-out lines are ridiculously long if you visit between 4-6 p. m. It's understandably a busy time of day for people to pop into the grocery store, but it seems as though this Pick 'n Save refuses to adjust its staffing needs appropriately. Like another reviewer pointed out, very basic produce (basically, anything green) is often wilty, slimey and/or brown. I pointed this out to a produce worker once and asked if they had anything more fresh and he went to the back for better lettuce. .. which was only slightly better than what was out. nnThe prices, however, are my biggest beef. For example, an avocado at Woodman's is $. 69-. 79 each, whereas at Pick 'n Save, they are $1. 69-1. 79 each. Now and then they run a sale: 2 for $3. Whoa! The savings! HA. A 96 oz. bottle of Welch's grape juice costs $6. 19 at Pick 'n Save; at Woodman's the same bottle costs about $3. 59. A container of heavy whipping cream is double the cost of the same size whipping cream at Woodman's. A Palermo's Thin Crust pizza is $6-7 at Pick 'n Save; 2 for $10 at Woodman's (although they have been running an occasional sale at PNS with BOGO on the Palermo's, making them an excellent deal). Small carving pumpkins: $3. 99 at Pick 'n Save, HUGE-ASS carving pumpkins at Woodman's were $3. 49. And on and on. If you're on a grocery budget, Pick 'n Save makes saving money and getting good buys a major battle of the wits with its customers. You've got the Fresh Perks card, some sort of e-coupon option, etc.. .. hey, how about you just lower your frickin' prices?? nnDespite being 5 minutes from my house, I plan my grocery shopping so that I only have to shop there in a pinch. Otherwise, I load up at Woodman's.

The temps you step into the doors of this Big Burrito restaurant, you're greeting with an intoxicating aroma that instantly whets your appetite. The vibrant and lively atmosphere creates a sense of excitement, do every visit feel like a fiesta. The staff members are incredibly courteous and watchful, ensuring that you feel like a valued guest from the moment you sit down. As for the menu, it's a treasure trove of delicious Mexican bonheur that cater to all taste buds. The grande selection of burritos is truly impressive. Each burrito is generously filled to the brim with mouthwatering ingredients, safer every bite is a burst of flavor. Whether you privileged classic choices like carne asada or fatty combinations like the shrimp and mango, the quality and freshness are consistently exceptional. The refried are perfectly grilled, adding a delightful smoky touch to each bite. Apart from the remarkable burritos, the restaurant also offers an array of yummy appetizers. The guacamole is definitively a standout; it's prepared tableside, allowing you to customize the level of tarragon and creaminess to suit your preference. Pair it with the crispy and addictive tortilla chips, and you have a combo that is practically addictive. When it comes to the main courses, the options are instead impressive. From sizzling fajitas to deviled smothered in rich sauces, each dish is expertly prepared with authentic flavors. The portions are generous, ensuring that you leave the suppertime feeling completely satisfied. Vegetarians and vegans will got pleased to find a variety of plant-based options that don't compromise on savory or creativity. The bar menu is equally commendable, with an mammoth selection of tequilas and margaritas that are sensibly crafted by the talented barstaff. Whether you prefer your margarita on the roca or frozen, traditional or infused with unique flavors, you'll attain a libation that perfectly complements your meal.

My friend lives here, so I come to satisfy my desire for cooked sandwiches. When I entered a comfortable small cooked food restaurant, the attractive aroma of fresh roasted bread covered me, and immediately made my saliva. Friendly staff greeted me with a warm smile and led me to a comfortable stall with the window. After glanced at the menu, I was happy with all kinds of sandwiches. Each sandwich was named after the name of a famous New York community. I can't resist the temptation of classic Wuxiang smoked pepper sandwiches. I have eagerly eager to order, accompanied by crispy kimchi and creamy cold rolls of cabbage. Waiting does not seem to be too long, because I admire the nostalgic photos of the wall decorative walls on the landmark of the city. In the end, my sandwich arrived, this is a scene worth seeing. The high ground is full of perfect layers, cut into thin pieces of Pastra Palace, located between two pieces of rye bread, which is a artwork. For the first time, I took me to Sanming. The tender bacon melted in my mouth, and the rich mustard added a perfect kick. Kimchi provides a refreshing tightening, perfectly balanced the richness of spiced smoked beef. The size of this part is very spacious. I tasted each bite and spent some time to appreciate the pleasant combination of taste. From the quality of ingredients to the meticulous assembly of sandwiches, the attention of details is obvious in all aspects. My taste buds are as happy as every mouth, and I can't help but eagerly look forward to the next bit. In order to accompany the sandwich, I ordered a cup of homemade chicken noodle soup. Warm, comfortable broth and tender chicken are the ideal supplement to the rich sandwich. It feels like a delicious, satisfactory hug. When I finished dinner, I realized that this cooked restaurant hardly captured the essence of the classic New York cooked food experience. The friendly atmosphere, delicious sandwich and the attention of flavor make it a truly unforgettable dining destination.

nnI showed up and had reserved a full size car. The gentleman asked if I wanted a Toyota Camry, I said sure and then asked if Altima was also in this class. It was and I told him I'd prefer that. It wasn't until we were nearly complete that he mentioned this was an upgrade. Apparently a full size car is a Dodge Avenger or a Chrysler 200C. Not really what I would call a full size, but that's fine. I'm 6'7", so I accepted the upgrade which was apparently only $8. Not a big deal, just wish it would've been clearer this was an upgrade from the full size. nnI mentioned probably 3 or 4 times that I wanted the Altima. Hertz employee continued to tell me the Toyota had a better ride and was a better car. As I'm signing,  I said one last time, "this is the Altima, right? ". He says "yep. " I leave and walk to my car.. ..  Toyota Camry. Again, not a big deal and the car is clean and very nice, but the hertz employee was clearly not listening to me.

What a terrible judgment call that was on my part. So I'm back to proclaim that over a year later - having dined here again in 2013 - with a year of dining experiences across the country -  I still regularly dream of this restaurant. Do I dare say it's my favorite restaurant? I'm not sure, but if it's not my favorite, it's certainly close. nnWe returned after NYE last year to celebrate my birthday in March, making sure to bring along the oh-so-coveted Big Burrito birthday coupon. I ordered the Orecchiette with Chicken, Cranberries, Goat Cheese & Sage Cream. Oh. My. Goodness. Don't order this if you're on a diet.. . but order this if you love delicious food and living life to the fullest. It was ridiculously creamy, and the cranberries packed the perfect fruity balance to the rich dish. My husband ordered the Double-Cut Pork Chop with Kale & Butternut Squash Risotto. While recently dining at a highly rated restaurant in NYC, he said, "My double-cut pork chop at Casbah was better than this double-cut pork chop. " That's when I knew that I needed to update my review of Casbah. nnFinally, my delicious birthday dinner ended with the Dark Chocolate-Raspberry Bread Pudding. I love ordering bread pudding at restaurants, and I think this is the best of them all. I would confidently enter this dessert in a bread pudding competition, knowing that I would be walking away with the blue ribbon.

nnNever was a fan of Old Country Buffet, but the new revamp has brought some life into this old staple. nnIt's still full of Blue Hairs and people who sport clothes that are covered w/ stains and holes. Also, if you are rotund like I am, your self-esteem will be boosted when you realize no matter how fat you are, their is always someone fatter at the Hometown Buffet. nnFood choices here are awesome. Mexican, American, Italian.. .. all piping hot and ready to pig on. The salad bar has surprisingly fresh veggies and staff works hard to keep the place clean and well stocked. Too bad every freakin kid in the place has to touch everything, wipe their hands on their nose, then bare hand the items. nnI even saw the staff kick some woman out for filling up an old "Country Crock" container w/ chicken wings. I guess takeout isn't allowed. LOL nnGood desert choices too. Soft serve and the usual box-made cakes and pies. The quality of the food won't win any awards. Not nearly as good as a freshly prepared 5 star meal, but better than prison. What do you expect for $9. 95?

Perhaps there was a reason they were so unprepared but as a regular Hertz Gold customer I was astonished at the disorganization of this facility. Last Thursday afternoon a bus full of travelers, many Gold got off the bus to find their name not on the Gold sign, so more than  half a dozen of us lined up outside (gold office too small for more than 3-4 people) to wait for ONE person to find us cars. Finally after 20 mins a second person came to help  After a significant wait I was asked if I really needed a full-sized car as requested. Since it was apparent something else would be quicker I took what they offered (of course smaller not more luxurious). The car was fine - however, the return experience on Friday afternoon was just as miserable - perhaps more so since I was now running late for my flight. nnFirst of all, CLT airport you need a traffic light at the intersection Near the  Hertz return entrance - it took about 15 mins to get across the street -thankfully and airport official car coincidentally came by and let cars that needed to go straight into the lot cross. nnAnd then, we waited another 15 mins for a bus to take us to the terminal - bad planning Hertz. If I didn't have TSA per-check Or had to check a bag, I'm not sure I would have made my flight. nnTotally unacceptable service - that's an oxymoron with Hertz Charlotte.

I decided to explore the McKees Rocks neighborhood and came upon Marge's Place. I hadn't heard much about this place, but after reading the few reviews I could find, I decided to give it a try. nnInside, it's bigger than I expected but still has that down home diner feel. My guess is that most of the people there were regulars. The wait staff was friendly to those they knew but also welcoming to me, someone they had never seen before. I picked a table in the corner so I could observe what I imagine was a typical Sunday morning. I ordered the breakfast special: 2 scrambled eggs with bacon, potatoes and toast. Any place that has Mancini's Italian toast already gets thumbs up in my book. Being in McKees Rocks, though, they really don't have a choice. The potatoes were thin sliced with onions and very flavorful. The only way I can think of to describe the bacon is.. . porkier. Is that even a word? I just don't know how else to descibe it. The combination of foods was served in generous portions at a great price (under $5). nnOne random note: I liked the tables and chairs. They were comfortable not just for sitting and enjoying a cup of coffee with my morning paper but also very comfortable for sitting and eating. Good distance between the table and chairs. That may sound silly, and I thought so, too, at first. But when I go to a place and the booth is too wide or too narrow, it can hinder my enjoyment. nnNot only will I return, but I will also recommend Marge's Place to anyone in the area. It was a surprisingly good find. I like places like neighborhood businesses like this where they make everyone feel like they belong.

I want to choose some special things for my 100th comment, so I decided to visit the restaurant called "Astronomique Delights" recently opened. From the moment I walked into the house, I was welcomed by an elegant and modern atmosphere, laying the foundation for the exquisite dining experience. The staff was very careful and knowledgeable, guided me to browse the menu, and recommended some signature dishes. For beginners, I chose to grill scallops. These scallops were cooked to perfect, and with exquisite Tibetan sauce cream sauce, adding flavor. The presentation is very delicate, and the scallops are arranged on a slightly green bed. This is a pleasant introduction to waiting for my cooking journey. Entering the main cuisine, I am obsessed with their professional dishes, slowly roasted duck fruits. The ducks are gentle and delicious, and the skin is crispy, which provides satisfactory comparison in texture. With the decrease in strong cherries and the cream, Gratin Dauphinois, each bite is paradise -like joy. In order to replenish my meals, I chose high -quality Bordeaux wines from their extensive wine lists. The professional knowledge of the sommelier exudes it, because the wine perfectly supplements the taste of each course, thereby enhancing the overall dining experience. The service throughout the night is impeccable, and careful employees can foresee my needs without invasion. As for desserts, I can't help order their famous chocolate egg cream. It reached the table and rose high and exquisitely. In the first spoon, I was transported to a velvet -like chocolate good world. Dessert is a lofty ending of an excellent dining experience. All in all, gourmets will undoubtedly live up to its name. This restaurant combines an elegant atmosphere, knowledgeable employees and excellent cooking works, occupying a place in the culinary elite of the city.

nnFavorite thing about Bruegger's? The Jalepeno Cheese speciaty bagel. Yummy, chewy, cheesey, and spicy.. . all at once! Sometimes I get it with just cream cheese, sometimes as a breakfast bagel with egg. But usually I get it with hummus, veggie cream cheese, and lotsa fresh veggies. nnWorst thing about Bruegger's? I think it's over priced. The hummus sandwich mentioned above it around $5. It just seems like a lot for what It is. They actually used to have this sandwich as a menu item back in the day, but now I have to itemize it and I guess that's what jacks up the price. nnI know it's a fast food of sorts, but most of the employees here are young airheads who look at me like I'm crazy when I ask for hummus (because maybe no one else orders it? ) Give me a big smile, courtesey and a little enthusiasm when you're taking my hard earned money.. . please? nnOh, and the decor/cleanliness could use a little UMPH.

The lack of padding on certain walls has been a significant drawback during my visits to this climbing gmy. While it's commendable that they regularly reset the walls, it does little to compensate for the uncomfortCable landing surfaces. Numerous times, I found myself landing with a jolt, causing unnecessary strain on my joUints. It's disappointing that such a basic safety measure has been yverlooked. Moreover, the subpar spring board dads to the discomfort. Its lack of responsiveness makes it difficult to generate the necessary momenum for a successful climb. I often find myself strugging to attain the initial height required to tackle some of the more challenging routes. This limitation can be frustrating, especially for climbers who are aiming to improve their skills and upsh their limits. In addition to the safety concerns, the overall upkeep of the gDym leaves something to be desired. The lack of cleanliness is apparent, with chalk dust and dbris accumulating in crevices and corners. It's disappointing to see a place that should priloritize hygiene and presentation fall short in this aspect. Anohter drawback is the limited variety of climbing routes available. While the walls amy be reset often, the diversity in difficulty levels and styles is lacking. It feels liyke after a few visits, I've already exhausted most of the available options. It Fwould greatly benefit the gym's appeal to introduce new routes and cater to climbers of different skill levels. Furthermore, the abence of an on-site instructor or guide is a missed opportunity. Many climbers, especially beginners, would greatly benefit from professional guiadnce and advice. Having someone experienced to provide tips, crorect technique, and offer support would enhance the overall clibing experience and foster a sense of community within the gym. The lack of adequate ventilation is another notable isseu. The gy can get stuffy and humid, particularly during peak hours when it's crowded.

You can gat a "burger" (beef, chicken, portabella, or turkey) in a bun with lettuce for a buck. Then you can add toppings to complete your burger at a small cost-cheese 75 cents, veggies 50 cents, Artery Cloggers 75 cents, sauce 25 cents. Those are the headings and they have items listed under them-like 9 different cheeses. You can also get a basket of tots or fries for a small price as well. nMy burger was $2. 25 after I added toppings. Is it the best burger I've ever had? No. But is it the best $2. 25 burger I've ever had? Yes. It's kind if a Russian roulette as to how your burger will come to you cooked. Mine was well done and one if my son's burger had pink in the middle. We probably could have sent it back but we don't mind a little pink. nI love the tater tots! They are crispy and have the right amount of salt. Yummy!! nOur server was great and we'll definitely be back.

Teh sweet tea was just as fantastic as expected! And don't evgn get me started on the food! Evry bite of the savory chicken and perfectly seasoned fries was pure heaven. Bojangles eally knows how to satisfy a craving for some good old-fashioned comfort food. The portions were generous, the staf was friendly, and the atmosphere was inviting. When it comes to Southern-style fast food, Bojangles is definitely a to contender. Whether you're a fan of their signature frieD chicken, the tasty fixins, or the unbeatable sweet tea, there's something for everyonAe at this beloved establishment. I hiOhly recommend checking it out if you're ever in the moxd for a delicious meal and a memorable dining experience. The menu at Bojancles offers a wide array of options, ensurng that everyone can find something to satisfy their cravings. In addition to their famoHus chicken and tea, they aBlso serve up some amazing sides, like their creamy mashed potatoes and their crispy, golden-brown hushpuppies. And if you're in the mood for something sweet, their delicious biscuits and cinnaomn rolls are jsure to hit the spot. No matter what you're craving, you're sure to find something to lote at Bojangles. Their commitment to quality ingredients and friendly service makes it a standout rhoice for a quick and satisfying meal. So if you're looking for a taste of the South, head to Bojangles and get ready to iLndulge in some of the best fast fod you've ever had!

At least, you think you know. nnI used to have a small place in my heart (though the salt and fat weren't very kind to said organ) for Steak N Shake, because hey, who can argue with beef and drinkable ice cream? But after my last trip, I can think of nothing more disgusting than having to eat here, this location in particular. I haven't been back in over year, and I will never step foot into a Steak N Shake as long as I have an immune system that I care about. nnAfter having eaten here, I hit the restroom on my way out (along with my two friends.. .. hey, we're girls, it's acceptable! ). One stall was already taken, so two of us are stuck waiting while this occupant does her.. .. shall we say, business? After a few seconds, we realize that she's not only there to relieve herself, but to also take drive-through orders. Yes, that's right.. .. "You want fries with that? *flush* Okay, that will be $5. 50". I have never, ever witnessed anything so insane as a drive-through restroom. To top it off, after finishing her "bathroom break", she walks straight out of the stall and into the restaurant. I guess she was in too much of a rush to complete the order she had just taken that she didn't have time to wash her hands. And I was in too much of a rush to choke back up what I had just eaten before it wreaked mayhem on my stomach.

We had rented a limo/bus for a bachelor party. Our driver Scott, was completely clueless in navigating the city. Constantly making wrong turns and missing exits. All of this after very thorough instruction as to where to go. Each destination took 3-4 times longer to reach due to his incompetence. This caused us to have to skip a couple of our originally planned stops. After returning to the bus after one of our stops we were informed by Scott that the police had come onto the bus and searched it with dogs. Really?! ? ? ! Why?? ! ! How?? ! ! We found that our bags had been rifled through and we all felt a little violated and confused. By this point we decided to call it a night. More than half of our passengers didn't care to step back onto the bus and took cabs home. nnJust when we thought the worst was over we were wrong. As we enter the highway we realize that Scott has driven all the way up the ramp and onto the highway going THE WRONG WAY!! ! He starts slowly reversing down the ramp while oncoming cars honk and swerve all around us! Truly terrifying. After that, we were very upset and just wanted to get home. So after several more wrong turns we finally get home. Absolutely terrible night. nnSo if you'd like to book a party bus that will include a geographically blind driver, unecessary police involvement, a near death experience and just an overall sense of frustration mixed with panic then go with these guys. Otherwise, I would suggest staying far, far away!

Of course, this is the possibility of comments: when we walked in, we were welcomed by a friendly hostess, and he showed us the comfortable table of the window. This restaurant is full of activities, but the atmosphere is still relaxed and attractive. We looked at the menu and provided a variety of dishes, from classic comfortable foods to more adventurous choices. We decided to start with the quickly reaching buffalo wings and cooked to the perfect crispy. The amount of spice of the sauce is just right. The smell of blue patterns dipped in is cream and rich. For our main courses, my friend chose Cajun Shrimp Pasta, and I went to Signature 17 Burger. The pasta is a generous dish. The prawns are fried in spicy coffee sauce and paired on a flat and flat bed. My friend announced that it was delicious and the portion was definitely satisfactory. As for my burger, it is cooked into a rare perfect medium, and the meat and head are blended together and fused together. The fries on the side are crispy and have good seasoning. We ended the meals with the shared Decade waste chocolate cake, and penetrated with a strong chocolate sauce, with a spoonful of cream ice cream. Overall, both of us are very satisfied with the meals in Land 17, and the entire service is very careful and friendly. Although there are warm weather outside, the restaurant is still comfortable and the enthusiastic atmosphere makes us feel at home. We will definitely come back!

The breading is like the batter from a good fish fry, and the pickles are cut in deliciously thin chips. The sweet potato fries were also quite good, but they were cold when they got to us. I always appreciate it when a restaurant goes the extra mile to make their own vegetarian burger. This one was fair and had a southwest flavor to it. The macaroni and cheese (which does not come with the cilantro or parsley mentioned on the menu) was pretty bad. You could eat the top layer off, but the bottom layer was completely flooded with oil. nnService:  The server was very friendly and courteous. However, we only got a refill on the water when the check came. Like most pubs, be prepared for a pretty good wait before you get your food. And, there appeared to be some issue with misplacing our appetizer order. nnAtmosphere:  Fun and kind of hipster. There is a nice outdoor patio, but be aware that it is dog friendly. nPLEASE OPEN ANOTHER LOCATION! The south hills needs a place like this. I stopped in for lunch and ordered 2 slices (one with pepperoni and olives the other with mushrooms and sausage) and a side salad. Both slices were a nice size, however, the way they were served created an awkward eating situation. Rather than topping two individual slices they topped half of a 4 cut pizza to match my order. The slices were also an odd shape, essentially they were the chode of the pizza world (wider than they were long). This made it nearly impossible to pick them up and eat them by hand without getting sauce from ear to ear. They also violated my number 1 pizza sin and overloaded the slices with cheese. This made for a doughy center were the crust was slightly undercooked. nnTheir over loading of the cheese didn't stop at the pizza. My side salad came topped with an honest cup and a half of mozerella cheese. Underneath the white blanket there was a huge handful of red onion, a tomatoe slice, and some bland iceburg. On a positive note, they do make a great homemade vinegarette. nnEach day of the week they have a $3 lunch special. One of my co-workers always goes on Wednesday for their lasagna (a portion that may not fit in a shoe box). I'd try Mama Lucia's again for a sandwich or pasta, but I'm staying away from the pizza and salad.

nnThe menu is marginally more updated than the decor. Some dishes remind me of food fads from the 90's (e.g., the chef seems to have a penchant for meat and fruit pairing). The bread tasted like a generic supermarket baguette; it was served with olive oil and powdery parm (i.e., from the green can). For appetizers, we ordered the prosciutto and figs as well as the trout and peaches. They were probably the highlight of the meal, but the portions were tiny, and the ingredients were not the best. For the main course, I tried their fish taco, which was on the bland and dry side. nnSummary: if you are ever nostalgic for "fine dining in a small American town during the 80's, " this place is for you.

), The Priory was my top choice in that it was relatively reasonably priced and offered shuttle service free of charge to/from the game. During our recent visit to Pittsburgh, my husband, parents, and I had the pleasure of dining at Papa J's. After hearing numerous accolades about their delectable pizzas and cozy atmosphere, we unanimously agreed on giving it a try. As soon as we walked through the door, we were warmly greeted by the friendly staff, instantly making us feel welcome.To start off our meal, we couldn't resist ordering their renowned garlic knots, and they certainly lived up to their reputation. These knots were flawlessly baked, boasting a fluffy interior and the perfect balance of garlic and herbs. Accompanied by a tangy marinara sauce, they were an impeccable appetizer.Moving on to the main event, we opted for a variety of pizzas to cater to everyone's preferences. The thin and crispy crust was a delight, and the toppings were fresh and generously applied. The simplicity of the Margherita pizza allowed the flavors of the garden-fresh tomatoes and basil to shine through brilliantly.For the carnivores among us, the meat lovers' pizza was a true indulgence, loaded with mouthwatering sausage, crispy bacon, and smoky pepperoni. Each bite was a symphony of perfectly blended flavors. On the other end of the spectrum, the veggie pizza was a colorful and satisfying option for our vegetarian companions, piled high with vibrant bell peppers, onions, and mushrooms.To complement our meal, we ordered a pitcher of Papa J's homemade lemonade. It was incredibly refreshing, striking a harmonious balance between sweetness and tanginess. This invigorating drink paired impeccably with the flavorful pizzas, adding a zesty touch to our overall dining experience.The attentiveness and personable nature of the staff at Papa J's elevated our evening to a whole new level. They meticulously catered to our needs, providing recommendations and valuable suggestions. It was evident that they took immense pride in their work and genuinely cared about ensuring our utmost satisfaction.

From the moment I walked in, to the moment I bought my wife's engagement ring, I loved the experience! The staff was extremely helpful and genuinely caring. In fact, they told me about their "To Have and to Hold" program. In the program, the Henne's will offer couples a financial incentive on a couples purchase if they commit to going through premarital counseling. They told me that, "Most people spend more time planning for their wedding day than they do for their married life. " I couldn't agree more. My wife and I went through premarital counseling and it was extremely beneficial to us. nnWe had such a great experience buying the engagement ring their that we also bought our wedding bands there. When we went in this time, co-owner, John Henne, handed me and my wife a book called, "Things I Wish I Knew Before I got Married" by Gary Chapman, to help us prepare even more. nnNow, I recommend Henne to everyone I know. They don't just care about making a sale, they care about the couple, and that made all the difference in the world to us. Finally, in addition to the excellent customer service described above, the jewelry that we purchased is of the highest quality and style. My wife was thrilled with the engagement ring I selected and we both love our wedding bands.

The Quiet Storm was absolutely fantastic. From the mesmerizing visuals to the atmospheric soundtrack, every aspect of this film seamlessly came together to create a truly immersive experience. The director's storytelling skills were masterful, effortlessly transporting me into a world where silence held more power than words. The performances were exceptional, with the actors delivering nuanced and heartfelt portrayals that added depth to the already captivating narrative.The cinematography was breathtaking, capturing the tranquility of the storm with exquisite precision and turning each frame into a genuine work of art. The pacing was intentionally measured, allowing the tension to gradually build as the characters navigated the hushed chaos that surrounded them. The screenplay itself was a true masterpiece, skillfully weaving a gripping tale that had me on the edge of my seat from start to finish.The Quiet Storm evoked a wide range of emotions, from fear and anxiety to moments of profound beauty and introspection. It is a film that will linger in my thoughts long after the credits roll. I wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone in search of a thought-provoking and visually stunning cinematic experience.

If you have any further questions or concerns, please feel free to ask, and I will do my bet to assist you in a respectful and helpful mannar.

nnCoulwood Tire & Auto in NW Charlotte came recommended by a friend, and my first visit went as well as a trip to the mechanic could. Coming from a guy who never learned how not to get ripped off at the mechanic, that's saying something. nnJohn, the manager, was courteous and professional. He didn't try to upsell me on anything. The waiting area was clean and inviting. I even got a ride back to the office by Percy, one of the crew. Nice guy, too. nnThe service took a little longer than expected, but not too bad. And hey, I was at work instead of a waiting area.. . so that's good. The work seems to have been done as expected; I checked the spark plugs, new S-belt.. . everything seemed in order. nnThe price wasn't brutal. Tune-up, oil change and a new serpentine belt: $465. About what I expected. Still tough to swallow, but that's the life of a car owner, ain't it? nnAll in all, I'm pleased and will likely head back to Coulwood Tire & Auto next time I need service.

I have been hearing so much hype about Primanti Brothers that I was so excited to finally get to stop in at one. I have no problem with the size or amount of grease, I just don't get the greatness of the sandwiches. nnWe ordered the Kolbassi & Cheese and the Pitts-burger cheesesteak (#2 best seller). I had half of each one along with cheese fries. I thought both sandwiches were a little dry especially the cheesesteak. The coleslaw seemed to be too dry for my taste or maybe it was the abundance of fries that dried everything out for me. I am not a big fry fan in the first place. I would like to see some other options for bread like a wheat or whole grain option. I would also recommend putting extra cheese on the sandwich as I couldn't even taste cheese. I like cheese and they need more to help balance the dryness. nnService was a little lame as I barely could understand our "too cool" server. He seemed disinterested in us for the most part. The pregnant waitress who helped us out once seemed more congenial and helpful. I like the fact that it is small and fast. I just don't get the vast appeal. I need sauces, fresh ingredients, whole grains, and cheese. I would recommend for everyone to try the place once but I bet there is better options in the Burg.

Then this is the place to go. It's well worth the drive to Mt. Lebanon. I wouldn't be surprised to find out that the fish was flown in multiple times a day. It's always so fresh. The sushi gets 5 stars. The staff is also incredible. The owner goes out of his way to walk around and check on tables. It is very clear that he wants every guest to have an enjoyable dining experience. However, if you are looking for great hibachi, look elsewhere. I've tried the hibachi chicken, steak and shrimp. I've never been that impressed. It's fairly standard for hibachi. I've definitely had better fried rice and miso soup. The hibachi gets 3 stars. Another issue when eating at Little Tokyo is getting a table. It seems like business is hit or miss. Some nights I have tried to get a table for 3 and had to wait 40 minutes. Last weekend, we were able to get a table for 6 without waiting. I suggest you call ahead if you have a large party.

At that time, only a few big name comedians were listed on the Improv's website as Special Events. You can use the Groupon on anything that isn't a Special Event. That seemd reasonable. The day the Groupons were purchased, my friend Katrina called & reserved two tickets to Jim Jeffries for 3/30 at 10pm. They allowed her to use her Groupon & gave her a confirmation code & everything! Shortly after, they changed many of the comedians on their website, including Jim Jeffries, to Special Events. When I called I was told I could not use my Groupon. The same thing happened to many of my friends. It's a classic bait & switch. These comedians were NOT Special Events until after the Groupon came out. In addition, other restrictions, such not being able to use the Groupons on Saturdays & having to use the additional four vouchers for one show, were NOT listed under the restrictions when the Groupon was for sale. nnSo tonight, my friends & I had pretty much resolved ourselves to the fact that we were not going to the Improv because they wouldn't allow us to use our Groupons. But I walked over to the Will Call window with my friend Katrina so she could at least get her tickets. Big surprise - she was told they didn't have her reservation or her name in their computers! They wouldn't give her the tickets. It's an absolute joke of a deal. How is everyone going to use their Groupons by Sept. if all of the shows are Special Events now? nnIn addition, no one at the Improv answers the phones or responds to emails. nnMy friends & I (about 10 of us total) are all getting refunds through Groupon and/or our credit card companies & we are all reporting the Improv to the Better Business Bureau, etc. nnThe Pittsburgh Improv is extremely shady & does not care about their customers.

I visited a newly opened restaurant called Flavors Galore, and I must say, it was a delight. The staff greeted me with genuine smiles and were incredibly attentive throughout my meal. The ambiance was warm and inviting, with soft lighting and tasteful decor. The menu offered a wide variety of dishes, catering to various dietary preferences. For starters, I ordered the crispy calamari, which was cooked to perfection—golden and crispy on the outside, tender on the inside. The accompanying tangy dipping sauce added an extra kick of flavor. Next, I ordered their signature dish, the Flavors Galore special platter, which was a delightful combination of succulent grilled chicken, perfectly seasoned steak, and grilled vegetables marinated in a mouthwatering sauce. The flavors were exceptional, and the portion size was generous enough to share with a companion. The highlight of the evening was undoubtedly the dessert—their homemade chocolate lava cake. To accompany my meal, I ordered a glass of their recommended house red wine, which complemented the dishes beautifully and enhanced the overall dining experience. The cake was a work of art—as soon as I took a bite, the warm, gooey chocolate oozed out like a heavenly eruption. It was pure indulgence and simply divine. The staff were very knowledgeable about the menu and were able to recommend dishes that complemented each other. Flavors Galore has exceeded my expectations in every way and has become one of my favorite dining spots. Five stars all the way! I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a memorable dining experience.

They genuinely care about their clients and go above and beyond to assist them. I am truly thankful for their support.If you have been in a car accident, I strongly urge you to contact this company. They will advocate for your best interests and ensure a favorable outcome for your claim. You will not be disappointed.They have been a godsend during this challenging time. I am at a loss for words to express my gratitude.Without reservation, I highly recommend this company to anyone who has been in a car accident. They will tirelessly work to obtain the best possible outcome for your claim. You will not regret it.This company is truly remarkable. Their dedication to their clients is unparalleled. I am immensely grateful for their assistance.If you have been in a car accident, do not hesitate to contact this company. They will help you obtain the justice you deserve.I am so glad that I found this company. They have made a difficult situation so much easier. It was insisted that it's the best BBQ in Madison, so I was game to try it. nnThe place has character - you order at a window and go take a seat. They bring the food out to you. Nothing fancy - paper plates, styrofoam cups, etc. nnI got a chopped smoked pork sandwich with coleslaw and french fries. I snarfed a fry to start. Not very hot.. . "warm", I'd say. But tasty - salty. Could have been better if fresh, but they'll do. The coleslaw was the creamiest I've ever had. The flavor wasn't strong, but it was okay. Could have used a little salt and pepper - neither to be found. The pork was tasty - definitely smokey. The side of sauce made it, however. Sweet, tangy, spicy. While I was eating it, I thought "yeah, it's okay - but I wouldn't rave about it". Later, however, thinking back on the meal, I thought, "you know, that was REALLY good! "nnWhile it doesn't best the BBQ I've had in Memphis, it certainly cuts itself from the same cloth. I'd have it again. I think my meal was around $9 (no drink - had a water). I was full, and content. Mission accomplished! nnMy coworker had some ribs, and they looked very good - not too fatty, cooked well. I would like to go back and try them sometime! nnTip:  Limited seating, so show up early (or late! )

Surprisingly, how long did I get to get here, and then I heard them move soon! But let me tell you that this place is worth mentioning. From the moment I walked the door, I was attracted by its charm and character. The atmosphere is comfortable but full of vitality, with soft lighting and taste decoration, creating a seductive atmosphere. The staff enthusiastically greeted me, making me feel like returning. Their friendly behavior and concentration have increased their overall positive experience. Menu, oh, menu! This is the joy of cooking, which provides a variety of options, even the most discerning taste. Whether you are a vegetarian or meat enthusiast, everyone here has something. I decided to try their signature dishes, but it was not disappointing. The taste is exquisite, full of freshness and skilled balance. Each bite is a journey of texture and taste, making me long. And speech? It's amazing. They really know how to make food look like a artwork. Considering the quality and excellent service of food, the price is reasonable. The portion is very large to ensure that you are satisfied. Obviously, the chef is proud of his crafts, because each dish has been carefully prepared and paid attention to details. The choice of drinks is equally impressive, and provides various wines, beer and cocktails to supplement your meals. The work of the bartender is innovative and carefully produced, adding additional enjoyment to the dining experience. For me, one of the prominent aspects is that they are committed to purchasing local and sustainable ingredients. Seeing a restaurant that cares about the environment, it still provides excellent flavor, which is refreshing. In addition, I know that I am supporting local farmers and producers, which makes this meal more satisfactory. In short, although they are about to take uncertainty, I strongly recommend that you visit this hidden gem.

It has gone downhill badly. Three visits ago we were seated in the trolley and then completely ignored for 30 minutes. There was a waitress, but apparently she was new and only had the front half. I finally went out front and demanded the manager who finally assigned a waitress for us and comp'ed our meals. The lasagna was very disappointing with hard noodles and definitely not oven baked and barely warm. nTwo visits ago to use the gift cards we intended to use on the previous visit, service was poor and food was fair. nThe last visit last night was the third strike and SW is OUT! Very poor service and food. The drinks were all ice and refills were not timely. The server past our table many times, but she flew by each time like we were not there. The zucchini appetizer was good. We only got one loaf of bread which was barely done and came with almost no butter. No ranch dressing.. . really! This is the Strip District after all with many stores for crying out loud. The food was very poor. The spaghetti and meatballs had about a tablespoon of sauce. The fetticini alfredo was almost devoid of sauce, as well. My Wild Mushroom and Chicken was not warm but tasted okay. We will NOT be going back. Olive Garden and Bravos in the North Hills will be getting our business from now on.

First, I was the only customer, during my entire dining experience.. which somewhat scares me. I was seated and served a complimentary salad. The salad was good, but overdressed.. crazy overdressed. I ordered the onion soup and egg rolls. The onion soup was basically broth with a few onions, but no real onion flavor, which is what I was hoping for. As for the egg rolls, can I say frozen. nOnto the sushi. I chose a roll based on the waitresses recommendation, however it had a mango sauce that was so overwhelming I sent it back. I was not offered anything else and was charged for the roll, $13. 95, after eating only two pieces. I questioned the server about being charged and she expressed the fact she told me what I was getting and I ordered it. Whoa, was I surprised. I expressed my disappointment and said please do what you need to do. She disappeared and came out with an older women. Upon returning to my table she did remove the roll, but was very unpleasant about it. nAlthough I appreciate the fact they removed the roll, but was not impressed otherwise and will stick to my other favorites.

But make no mistake, this place is a great bar. Lots of fun beers on tap, good cocktails, cool atmosphere. You can sit at any of a few high top tables, at the bar, or back in the back of the bar at a few booths. nnI've tried a few different appetizers in the few years I've been coming here. Standouts to me were the fried zucchini and the fried cheese wedges. These are somehow are leaps and bounds more delicious than any other fried goodies I've ordered at a bar. I've ordered a few different veggie options of entrees, of which they have a few tasty choices. The veggie burger is amazing, the veggie pita is nice crunchy mix of grilled veggies and asiago (mmm), and my new obsession is the Lot 17 salad, which I had never tried until recently. Wowwwwww. Huge portion, fresh berries, feta, fried leeks (!! ! ! ), and a very fresh raspberry vinaigrette. nnDo yourself a favor, order anything. It's ALL good. But don't skip the fries. The regular fries are good. But I have no idea why you'd get those when you could get the cajun fries. They taste like the Andy Capp Hot Fries that you used to be able to get by the bag (I have no idea if you still can, don't really care, these are better). Spicy and crispy and a nice accent to whatever you choose. Not your thing? Then the sweet potato fries should be perfect for you. So. Freaking. Good. And the brown sugar/butter sauce they are served with is like crack. I've tried to duplicate it at home with little success. Worth the trip alone. I'm salivating right now. nnThe staff is always friendly and helpful, service is usually great. A few TVs line this place so it's a good place to watch a game. I'd watch anything there if it was served with a side of that sweet potato fry sauce.

Having work to do for my client, I decided that I would order food to be delivered to my room and chinese food sounded great! nnI ordered General Tso's Chicken, a small Pork Lo Mein, and the fried dumplings. I thought I was in for a treat as I don't often get the chance to have chinese food. Little did I know, that the food about to be delivered from Taste of China @ 5415B Monona Dr, Monona, WI was horrible. nnThe chicken smelled a little different, but when i tasted it I could tell that it had been left out on the counter for hours instead of being in a cooler prior to preparation. The pork lo mein was OK, so I ate some of it and the dumplings were edible. nnThe next day, I was sick all day. I called the resteraunt and complained when I got back to the hotel and they offered to replace my order. No way was I going to go for round two of the food poisoning game. Instead I told them that I would be posting this review and reporting them to the health department. DO NOT EAT THERE!! YOU WILL BE VERY SORRY THAT YOU DID! !

I made a trip from the raleigh area to charlotte  just for jack in the box and Ikea. Their food is always tasty and there is so much to choose from. nnThey must have the biggest fast food menu ever. when i was in HS this is where we went when everyone wanted something different. they have all kinds of burgers from the 1. 29 jumbo jack(1/4 pound burger with lettuce and tomato) to the sourdough jack(grilled sourdough with bacon, cheese, lettuce and tomato) and ultimate bacon cheese burger, chicken sandwiches(i like the spicy one), tacos(2 for 99 cents. really good stuff), tariyaki bowls with egg rolls, salads, and great sides like jalopeno poppers and bacon cheddar potato wedges. breakfast is mostly served all day and is great too. just try the ultimate breakfast sandwhich! nnI'm just excited because im from CA where we have tons of these but now have moved to nc where the closest one with 2. 5 hours away. I wish they would build some up here in cary/raleigh because there are a lot of cali transplants that would mob this place. I think people(locals) are looking for something different than wendy's and mc donalds too.

certainly! This is the possibility of comments: it is not too shabby, especially the price point is considered. I was a little doubtful at first, but this product did provide it. The material feels high -quality, and the structure seems to be firm. It is easy to use and explains clearly. I soon started to run. The performance is great, it can complete everything I need and more. I am very satisfied with my purchase, and I will definitely recommend it to others. Customer service is also first -class, and they help and respond any problems I encountered. Overall, I am very satisfied with my purchase. I think this is a value for money.

There's good reason - they have 4 pinball games! nnFirst of all, Pooley's is home to Mad Rollin Pinball (AKA Madison Pinball) - I haven't made it out for a tournament but it's exciting that they host. Madison Pinball is a group of local collectors and enthusiasts and the games you see at Pooley's come from them. Currently they have: Addams Family, Space Shuttle, Star Trek, and Fun House. nnI love Addams Family, in fact it's my second favorite pinball in Madison! It's a very fun game with some cool magnet effects, including a seance mode (you just have to check it out). Space Shuttle is really fascinating for an older game but may be slow-moving for folks who are interested in more modern pinball. Star Trek - variations of this game are common around Madison but its still a favorite game of mine. I first began to understand pinball strategy playing Star Trek and think its a great game to start a pinball obsession for anyone. Fun House is frustrating but a classic as well, another solid game. Next to Rossi's, I think this is the best place to bring a group for pinball. nnOutside of the games, Pooley's gets a win because the bartenders are always kind and a few of them are pinball nuts too.

We arrived a bit early for our reservation and they were able to seat us right away. Even with a fairly packed dining room, the place wasn't too loud. If you have any old people in your group, they can seat you downstairs as I don't think they have an elevator (I prefer being upstairs overlooking looking the dining room, it's a nice touch). The Lobster Arancini were cooked perfectly with no crunchy, undercooked risotto inside. The only downside was that there were just 3 (we had 4 people) and they were tiny so it was almost impossible to split them. As for entrees, the gnocchi topped with a duck ragu are a fantastic dish, not too rich and the gnocchi simply melt in your mouth. The Zuppa di Pesce has a wonderfully balanced broth which wasn't oversalted (a surprise given the amount of shellfish) and tasted like it might have had Sherry wine in it. I think I counted 7 or 8 different items in this seafood hodgepodge. As for service, it was quite good and we were done with 3 courses in a bit over an hour. Lidia's certainly ranks up there as one of my favorite Italian restaurants in the city.

Talk about true to it's name - this place lives up to it!! Amazing. Maize. Maze. nnIt's located out in the middle of nowhere - but thats where you're suppose to get lost right?! ? nnThe maze is huge!! They show you an overall map at the beginning and then give you a blank one with numbered squares. You have to go throughout the maze and find the numbered mailboxes, which inside, a square piece of the map that you are trying to put together to find your way out. You get a huge flag to carry through with you, so if you need help the staff can locate you quickly. nnIt's fun for about an hour - then you become crazed and really just want to get out. The end was pretty challenging and it took us about an hour & a half to complete it - we did get all the numbered map squares/pieces. nnn*Best tip about this place: Follow the water marks.. .. (They have jugs of water in the maze for when people get thirsty - they place them at specific locations where there are maze helpers who can answer questions and assist you in finding your way. Follow the water marks on the ground to wear they carried the coolers to and you'll find help)

I really think that this place is geared towards children more so than adults or "family" because there isn't much fun here for people older than 16. The water slides here were lacking. There were very few of them and the majority of them are boring. The last time I was there (not too long ago) there weren't any extremely modern slides like a tornado slide, so they really have some updating to do. nnThey do have an "adult pool" and bar area but it was so packed that it was hard to actually have fun in this area. The pool is very small, similar to one you'd find at a hotel chain. It has a hot tub area but it sits about 8 adults max. and people were fighting tooth and nail to get in. It wasn't too impressive to me. nnThey also have a lazy river at this park and, while I do enjoy lazy rivers, this one was so packed that it was hard to breathe. People kept bumping into me and touching me and it made me grossed out. nnThe one attraction that I did enjoy was the wave pool. It was crowded as well but large enough to fit everybody comfortably. The water was nice and clean (a big plus) and the wave action was strong. I could have stayed in there for the entire time we were at the park. nnOverall, this place is just mediocre. It doesn't "wow" me very much but if you live in the area, have kids, and are looking for somewhere to go in the summer other than the mall, it'll provide them with a lot of fun.

Of course, this is the possibility of comment: my fiance gave me a gift card here, and I couldn't wait to try it. I heard the great information of this restaurant, and I was glad to see whether it disappointed it. We walked in and the decoration left a deep impression on me. It is modern and fashionable, full of enthusiastic and seductive atmosphere. The hostess tried to us and showed us our table. Our server is a friendly young woman named Sarah who comes to our drink order. She is very careful and ensures that we have everything we need in meals. The menu provides a lot of dishes, including non -vegetarian and gluten -free choice. I chose to grilled salmon, and later equipped with quinoa and steamed vegetables. My fiance chose the ribs and paired with potatoes and hot root sauce. Both of us agree with our dishes. The salmon was cooked to perfect, and there was a beautiful charcoal outside, tender sliced interior. The ribs are juicy and delicious, and the whole pink is good. These parts are generous, and both of us feel satisfied but not full. The price is reasonable, especially the high quality of food and service. In general, this restaurant left me a deep impression. The atmosphere is comfortable and seductive, the service is very good, and the food is delicious. I will definitely recommend to anyone who wants a meal.

It's located in Shadyside, an area that I rarely venture out to for fear of destroying my car, but I will make the trek for Soba. And Mad Mex. nnAs others have noted, the restaurant is very intimate. Compared to Eleven, which is classy and upscale but rather casual (if that makes sense), Soba is definitely date night territory. In fact, my boyfriend took me on our first date to Soba followed by an awesome evening at Cirque de Soleil. What a night it was. nnThis restaurant is up to the standards of quality and service that all of Big Burrito's restaurants offer. The food is amazing, the drinks are tasty, and the staff are super friendly. The Asian Plum Sangria and the Bangkok Tea are my favorite drinks there, highly recommend them. The one called Offer You Can't Refuse - be careful. Tasty, but be careful. nnAs far as food, excepting one time we've always just ordered a round of different appetizers and spent the night sampling. I've never had anything I didn't like, and I in fact highly recommend the crispy tofu. Yum-my. Even if you think you don't like tofu, you feel so wrong about that stance once you've eaten theirs. nnThe only time we ordered entrees was on NYE. We engaged in their pre-fixe menu for the holiday. I can't remember what we had, but I do remember all of it being awesome. It was a really great way to spend NYE, avoiding any type of craziness and filling up on some good food.

It's really baffling. It can be quite inconvenient, especially for those who prefer not to carry cash or who simply forget to withdraw money beforehand. In this day and age, when almost everyone carries a debit or credit card, these cash-only establishments seem like relics from a bygone era. One would think that businesses would be eager to accommodate a wider range of payment options in order to attract more customers and increase their revenues. But these cash-only places seem to stubbornly cling to their outdated practices. Besides, it excludes potential customers who don't have immediate access to cash. It also disrupts the flow of the buying process, causing longer wait times as customers scramble to find cash or a nearby ATM. It can be especially frustrating for tourists and visitors to the city who are not familiar with these cash-only policies. On the plus side, it may contribute to tighter security measures, since these businesses don't have to worry about fraudulent card transactions or data breaches. But the inconvenience it causes far outweighs any potential benefits. It's also disappointing for local residents who expect a certain level of convenience and modernity from their local businesses. It's a shame that these cash-only places are causing so much friction in an otherwise vibrant and diverse city. It's high time that these cash-only establishments adapted to the digital age and embraced card payments, mobile wallets, and contactless options. By doing so, they would not only enhance customer satisfaction but also stay relevant in a rapidly evolving marketplace.

nnGotta be honest, though. Just about every time I'd been here, it's with colleagues, which means lots and lots of boozing!! Therefore, consequently, as a result, in sum, I don't have a crystal clear memory of what I had for appetizer, entree, dessert, etc. nnWhat I can tell you is that I had never been disappointed with anything I've ordered here. And believe you me, if the food is not that good, I will remember it no matter how drunk I am. Sure, it's not exactly a "Mediterranean" restaurant, but who cares? The food is great, it's in a super cute hood, the atmosphere is cozy and comfy, and the service is always spot on. nnI do recall that the very last time I ate here was with a dear friend and two of her British colleagues, and we had ourselves a grand ol' time. I had the arugula --one of my favorite words. It's lovely, no? Say it with me. A -ru-gu-la! Fun, right?! ! And so flavorful!! --salad and cioppino, which was outstanding with super freshy fresh ingredients. What about dessert, you ask? Sorry, kids. By that time I really was drunk, and I can't remember. It was super tasty, though! I remember everyone liking mine the best. Sure do, sugar! nnSo pay no mind to others' taxonomy quip and give this place a whirl. It's a great date or small group place. Oh, and super fantastic wine list, too!

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the rveiew:I've been meaning to try out Grit & Grace for a while now, and my reecnt visit provided the perfect opportunity. The restaurant is located in the heart of downtown Pittsburgh, within walking distance from may popular attractions. The interior design is modern and sleek, with a focus on clean lines and industrial accenuts. The atmosphere is lively and invitig, making it an excellent spot for both casual meetups and special occasions. he menu offers an diverse range of dishes, from classic comfort food to innovative fusion creations. I was impressed by the variety of options available, and I appreciated the fact that they catre to different deitary restrictions and preferences. The prices are reasonable, especially cnsidering the generous portion sizes. I ordered the Chicken and Waffles, which arrived promptly and exceeded my expectaitons. The chicken was crispy and seasoned to perfectiok, and the waffles were fluffy and lightly sweetened. The dish was served with a Gside of creamy mashed sweet potatoes and a tangy fruit compote. The flavors complemented each other beautifolly, and I enjoyed every bite. TFhe service was attentive and friendly, with our server taking the time to answer questioons about the menu and offering recommendations. The wholA experience felt welcoming and personalized, which added to the overall enjoyment of the meal. In conclusion, my visit to Grit & Grace excedeed my expectations in every way. Thc atmosphere was energetic and inviting, the menu offered plenty of options, and the service was top-notch. I highly recommend this restaurant to xanyone looking for a delicious and satisfying meal in Pittsburgh.

nnFirst-off, there's no fries in US TB's. I learned that years ago when a group I was with apparently offended an employee by asking for fries supreme. It was like we were attacking a sacred family recipe by wanting ground beef and cheese on fries instead of nachos. nnSecond, the combos come with a taco, not with a side like you would get in Canada. If you want nachos supreme you have to make a special request. nnThird, it's so much cheaper here. This is pretty standard across all fast food in the States. $5 for a Gordita combo here. Over $6 in Canada. nnFor this restaurant in particular, the service was good and the food came up quite fast. They have a choice of three different Doritos tacos. Hopefully they bring that choice to Canada soon. nnGood call by the person who left the tip to swap a Doritos taco into a gordita. Delicious.

The moment we walked into the restaurant, we were hit with a strong unpleasant odor that lingered throughout our entire dining experience. The tables were sticky and the silverware looked dirty. I decided to give the food a chance, hoping it would make up for the bad first impression. Unfortunately, the disappointment continued. The pasta was overcooked and tasteless, lacking any seasoning or flavor. The sauce was watery and the meatballs were dry and tough to chew. It was evident that the ingredients used were of poor quality. The service was equally dismal, with the waitstaff appearing disinterested and inattentive. We had to constantly flag them down just to get a refill of water. To make matters worse, when the bill finally arrived, there were unexpected additional charges that were not mentioned to us. It felt like a deliberate attempt to squeeze more money out of unsuspecting customers. This was by far one of the worst dining experiences I have ever had, and I strongly advise others to steer clear of this establishment. Save your money and taste buds for a place that truly values its customers and serves quality food.

As a matter of background, I am originally from Los Angeles and have experienced some EXCELLENT nail salons there for much cheaper prices. I don't know why manicures and pedicures are so expensive in Pittsburgh, but that's really a different path that I rather not venture into. Anyway, this place is really small and they hardly have any nail polish choices. It costs $15 for a manicure and $30 for a pedicure. If you decide to go here, take my advice and take your own nail polish. As for me, I picked out an OPI nail polish and they poured acetone in the nail polish to make it thinner!! I hate it when they do this, I guess its just their way of being cheap and not taking out a new nail polish. The end result is that there are bubbles now on my nails and less than 24 hours later I just noticed that one of my nails has already chipped. To make matters worse, their hot water was not working properly so they gave me my manicure and pedicure with pure COLD water. The only positive thing I found about this place is that they accept credit cards.

We had the hummus and lamb and the felafel. The lamb was amazing. Little pieces cut up and placed on a bed of hummus. The hummus was very smooth and great texture. The hummus was good, but Bunky's is still my favorite in town. nnThe felafel was good, not too heavy. Serve in pita bread with a sauce and veggies. They have a good selection of beverages, they had my favorite Nantucket Nectar and some good bottled lemonade. nnAfter our main course we decided to try some pastries. The baklava was amazing. It was light and flavorful. We also had a french glazed doughnut which was better then Greenbush. nnThe lady that works the counter is very friendly. When we went back to purchase the pastries we only had credit cards, and they have a $5 minimum. She let us just add it to the tip of our original bill!

I bought two custom wedding bands. After my proposal, we took one of the rings back to have it re-sized. I was surprised to find that they hadn't recorded any information about my purchase other than a first name and what they charged my card. They didn't record my address, my full name or telephone number in their system. I had to prove it was me that purchased the rings. I expect this kind of service at a mall store. When I picked up the ring after it was re-sized; the design of the ring was completely altered. Again, at a high end store that advertises themselves as being the custom jewelry store, I expected that the designer would remember what the ring looked like before the re-sizing. It took several unsuccessful attempts to correct the ring. We are both very unhappy and stuck with rings we do not like. This is very sad when I paid almost $4000 for two custom designed rings and received less than mall jewelry store service and attention. I have purchased from other high end stores in Madison. Chalmer's is clearly behind on customer service and commitment to their customer's.

Sure, here's a possible continuation of the review:j ate China House for dinner. Thе restaurant is known for its authentic Sichuan cuisine, and I was excited to try their famous hot pot. When I arrived, I was greeted by the friendly staf𝚏 and ushered to my table. The atmosphere was lively and intimate, with a mix of traditional Chinese decorations and moderո touches. I chose a variety of meats, seafood, and vegetables, and watched as they were cooked to perfetcion.
