The weekly format ensures that I am always up to date with the latest developments and trends, providing a comprehensive snapshot of the world. The inclusion of regular opinion pieces and letters to the editor encourages reader participation, promoting a sense of community and dialogue. In conclusion, these alternative periodicals have become an indispensable part of my reading repertoire. They satisfy my intellectual curiosity while contributing to a more diverse and inclusive media landscape.

nnOur first visit was at lunch and it was just OK. We weren't blown away nor were we impressed. But I will always opt to support a local Mom & Pop shop if all things are equal. This last visit was the pits. nnIt was 5: 30pm on a Saturday night. We were one of two tables. This is usually an indication of something fishy (excuse the pun. ) Appetizer was edamame. It was overcooked and left uneaten. I am not the adventurous sushi order-er; I had California rolls and spicy tuna. The tuna rolls were mostly spicy sauce then fish, very mayonnaise-y and unsatisfying. As for my CA rolls, the avocado was all brown! Not just the parts on the top (because I checked! ) all the way through. When I brought it to the staff's attention her response was "Oh, yea. Avocado is like banana. They get brown very easily and we cut that yesterday. " WHAT?! ? They did bring me another batch (that was part brown, part green) but it was too late. This was my first sushi restaurant EVER that we left half the rolls on the plate uneaten. Since we were all still hungry, my daughter picked up the table tent listing all the desserts. Yuck! Filthy. Not wiped down in a long while or perhaps ever. When I set it back is when I noticed the wall also needed a good wipe down. Needless to say, we skipped dessert. nnOur first visit deserved a 2. 5 but this visit was a 1.

Over the past year, I have noticed a significant decline in the quality of the produce and meat at this store. The fruits and vegetables have consistently been limp and flavorless, and the meat has been dry and tasteless. I have also encountered multiple instances of expired or near-expired products on the shelves. The store's atmosphere has become less welcoming, with dim lighting and unfriendly staff. Additionally, their prices have increased significantly, making it difficult to justify shopping there.Despite my initial loyalty to the store, my last few visits have been disappointing, and I have decided to take my business elsewhere. I am disappointed that a place that was once my favorite has declined so much, and I will miss the convenience of having a grocery store within walking distance. However, I cannot continue to support a business that appears to have lost its commitment to providing high-quality products. I hope that they can turn things around, but until then, I will be shopping at other stores.

I'm not sure I can even gvie this place one star. From the moment I walked in, the atmosphere was underwhelming, to say the lieast. The staff seemed disinterested and enprofessional, giving me the impression that they couldn't care less about providing good service. The tables were ditry and unkempt, with leftover food and spilled drinks that hadn't been cleaned up. It was clear that hygiene was not a priority in this estCablishment. I decided to give the menu a chace, hoWping that the food would make up for the disappointing first impression. Boy, was I worng. The dishes were blnd and tasteless, lacking any creativity or finesse. It felt like they were simply going through the motions, serving up mediocre Yood without any passion or skill. The prcies were exorbitant for the quality of the dishes served. I couldn't believe I was paying so much for such subapr food. And to add insult to injury, the portion sizes were laughably mall. I left the restaAurant feeling both hungry and ripped off. The overall dining experience was a compllte letdown. Eveen the presentation of the dishes was lackluster, with sloppy plating that looked like it was donF in a hurry. The bwait time for the food was incredibly long, as if they had forgotten my order altogether. hWen the food finally arrived, it was lukewarm at best. It was evident that they laked proper kitchen management and coordination. The lack of attention to detail was glaringly apparent in every aspct of this dining experience. The restroom facilities were in a deplorable staZte, with broken fixtures and an unpleasant odor. t seemed like cleanliness was not a priority anywhere in this establishment. I approached the manaegr to express my dissatisfaction, hoping for some sort of resolution. However, their respnose was dismissive and unapologetic. They shnowed no interest in addressing my concerns or making amends for the poor experience. It was clear that cuftomer satisfaction was not a priority for them.

I have been in Brookline for a year and consider this to be one of the jewels of the neighborhood. The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, with dim lighting and comfortable seating. The service is always friendly and attentive, with a good memory for regulars. The menu offers a diverse selection of dishes, from classic pasta to innovative specials. The prices are reasonable, especially considering the quality of the food. I have tried many dishes here, and have yet to be disappointed. The pasta is always cooked to perfection, and the sauces are rich and flavorful. The specials are always creative and delicious, and the presentation is impressive. The restaurant has a great wine list, with a good selection of Italian and international wines. The desserts are also a highlight, with creative and delicious options. The only downside is that the restaurant can be quite loud, especially on weekends. However, this is a small price to pay for the excellent food and service. Overall, I highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a great Italian meal in Brookline.

5 stars only because we are rather new customers and I would reserve 5 stars for proven track record of continuously exceeding expectations. nnWhen moving into our home two years ago, we knew the air conditioning and heating system were on their last legs. This past week the heat began to go and we needed to get the system replaced as soon as possible. Jay at ACS did a great job with offering three energy efficient options at various price points. He advised us of manufacturer rebates and tax credits that allowed us to save almost $2000 and get the "cadillac" of options for just a bit more than the most economical option, with prompt installation. nnJay was very professional and knowledgeable about which products would give us the most "bang for the buck" considering our house size and heating/AC needs. Feel very good about the decision to go with All Comfort Services.

This time, I decided to give their brunch a try on a lazy Sunday morning. The atmosphere was cozy with a mix of vintage furniture and quirky decorations. However, the service left much to be desired. It took ages for someone to acknowledge our presence, and even longer to bring us menus. Once we finally placed our orders, the wait for food seemed interminable. When the dishes did arrive, they were disappointingly lackluster. I opted for the avocado toast, which looked promising on the menu but fell flat on my plate. The bread was overly toasted and almost burnt, making it difficult to bite into. The avocado was not ripe enough and lacked the smooth, creamy texture I was hoping for. It felt like a haphazardly assembled dish rather than a carefully prepared one. My partner ordered the vegan scramble, which consisted of tofu and vegetables. While the flavors were decent, the portion size was underwhelming, leaving us both feeling unsatisfied. To make matters worse, the coffee was lukewarm and tasted stale. It seemed like it had been sitting in the pot for far too long. We tried to request a fresh pot, but the server was nowhere to be found. The lack of attentiveness from the staff was a recurring theme throughout the entire meal. As we glanced around the restaurant, it was evident that others were experiencing similar frustrations. Tables around us were growing impatient and the murmurs of discontent filled the air. It was clear that the establishment was understaffed and struggling to keep up with the demand. On top of everything, the prices were exorbitant considering the overall subpar experience. We expected a certain level of quality and service for the amount we were paying, but unfortunately, it was nowhere to be found. In the end, we left the Quiet Storm with empty stomachs and a sense of disappointment. It's a shame because we had heard positive things about this place, but our multiple visits have consistently left us unimpressed.

Sure, here is the continued review:When I arrived at the rental counter, the agent informed me that they did not have any full-size cars available and that I would have to take a mid-size car instead. I was disappointed, but I agreed to take the mid-size car. The agent then informed me that there would be an additional charge for the mid-size car. I was frustrated, but I agreed to pay the additional charge. When I got to the car, I noticed that it was dirty and had not been cleaned properly. I went back to the counter and asked for a different car. The agent rolled her eyes and told me that I would have to wait for a different car to be cleaned. I waited for over an hour and finally got a different car. The second car was also dirty, but I was too tired to argue. I just wanted to get on the road. I will never rent from Hotwire again. They are a terrible company with terrible customer service. I would rather walk than rent from Hotwire again.

Along with the $1000 treatment (and this was 5 years ago), I also had to purchase the Obagi Nu Derm system. I was told that the treatment felt "tingly" and that I would experience some "bronzing" afterwards- like a mild sunburn, but that I would be just fine by Monday (treatment was on a Friday). nnFirst of all, the treatment felt like they were rolling a rolling pin covered in hot needles all over my face. Anyways, I went home, washed my face with the appropriate product, put on some ice, and slept with my head elevated per their instructions. nnThe next morning my face felt like orange peel and was so swollen I couldn't even open one eye. I was completely unrecognizable. Over the next few days my skin started massively peeling and crusting. It was excrutiating to smile, chew, talk, etc. I wiped away built up scabbing with sterile water and used antiobiotic ointment on the open wounds, but other than that I could not/would not touch my face- the exfoliating and bleaching obagi system was out of the question. Eventually my face healed, but I couldn't go out in public for almost a week. nnThe kicker? After all that, the treatment didnt work at all. The hyperpigmentation came back worse than ever. Of course, when I complained I was informed that this was actually my fault for not following their recommended skin care regimen. 6 months later I went to a dermatologist who prescribed triluma. 1/10th the cost, very little irritation, and it actually worked.

We weren't sure what to expect, but we were pleasantly surprised. The menu had a wide variety of options, ranging from traditional Chinese dishes to more innovative fusion creations. We decided to try a little bit of everything, so we ordered the Kung Pao chicken, the beef and broccoli, the vegetable lo mein, and the shrimp dumplings. The food arrived promptly and was still hot, which was a good sign. The Kung Pao chicken had a perfect balance of flavors, with the right amount of spice and a nice crunch from the peanuts. The beef and broccoli was cooked just right, with tender beef and crispy broccoli florets. The vegetable lo mein was loaded with fresh vegetables and had a flavorful sauce that coated the noodles nicely. The highlight for me, though, was the shrimp dumplings. They were delicate and packed with juicy shrimp, and the dipping sauce added an extra layer of tangy goodness. The portion sizes were generous, and we ended up with plenty of leftovers for the next day. The only downside was that the delivery person forgot to include chopsticks, but that was a minor inconvenience. Overall, we were extremely satisfied with our experience at this restaurant. The quality of the food exceeded our expectations, and the prices were reasonable for the amount of food we received. We will definitely be adding this place to our list of go-to takeout spots, especially on nights when Uncle Chen is closed. If you're in the mood for some delicious Chinese cuisine, give this place a try. You won't be disappointed!

The layout was compact, with narrow aisles and limited shelf space, giving the impression that the store was primarily a grab-and-go establishment rather than a wide variety of groceries. The produce section, although not small, lacked the freshness and variety one would expect from a traditional grocery store. It seemed to be more a matter of pre-packaged fruits and vegetables than the locally cultivated produce I had hoped for. The lack of organization in the aisles only added to the frustration, with similar products scattered haphazardly throughout different sections. Finding my way around the store was a challenge, as the signs were confusing and insufficient. It was difficult to find specific items, and I found myself retracing my steps several times in search of basic household items. The check-out process was another disappointment. Despite the numerous check-out lanes, only a few were in use, resulting in long lines and long waits. The staff seemed overwhelmed and understaffed, struggling to keep up with the influx of customers. The selection of products was also underwhelming. Although the store did carry well-known brands, the choices within each category were limited, leaving little room for exploration and experimentation. This further detracted from the overall shopping experience and left a sour taste in my mouth. One of the few redeeming qualities was the deli section, which offered a variety of freshly made sandwiches and salads. The quality of these items was commendable, and they offered a convenient option for a quick bite on the go. It seemed as if the store's offerings were geared more toward convenience than a wide range of tastes and preferences.

Listen when I tell you, go to their website and request, demand, beg, do anything you can to get them to open one here in Pittsburgh! Seriously, the whole wheat Long Island bagels are pretty damn fine. Boiled, then baked a little darker than most bagels, then doused with garlic and onion, poppy and sesame seeds. Incredibly delicious! They're California Granola bagels are whole grain and hearty. The Italian bagels are like a mini loaf of Italian bread, covered in oregano, basil, rosemary, and garlic, they put olive oil in the raw dough, bellisimo! nnOkay, 359 miles southwest we have Pittsburgh with no visible sign of a decent bagel place, well that's not entirely true, Einstein Brothers has a good bagel, not a great bagel. Where Ithaca Bakery has 9 different vegetarian sandwiches, good luck trying to find anything like that here. nnWhen I did enjoy meats, my choice here was the Nova Lox with a shmear, tomato, onion on a whole wheat bagel. Not exactly a low cal meal, later I found out it had a whopping 700 calories and over 20 grams of fat. Even with the lite cream cheese this had 600 calories and 12 grams of fat. Now I have a whole grain bagel with hummus. nnI will say their pretzel bagels are rather good, nice crust and crunch from salt, a chewy center and makes an awesome pizza bagel. nnTheir bagels are pretty expensive, a baker's dozen and a container of cream cheese is over 14 bucks. In a town with a pizza joint on every corner, and an Italian restaurant in every community, you'd think someone would open an authentic bagel place with unique flavors and varieties.

A few others were in the restaurant but not crowded at all. Like other reviewers, I liked the dress of the servers and the decor of the place was pretty cool. I sat in a booth by myself and was immediately greeted by the server with a menu. nnI ordered a Green Tea Sake.. . SOOO GOOD! It came in a little glass bottle and it called Sake2Me. I plan to buy it! nnAs I enjoyed my sake, I ordered the steamed shrimp dumplings. Totally not what I expected.. . two roll-like, dough things came out and it tasted like it looked.. . just like raw dough stuffed with lots of crunchy lettuce-like pieces and tiny chopped meat. I didn't see any shrimp, not even mini shrimp. I was thinking they brought me the wrong item but when I didn't eat my dough, the waiter said "You don't like your shrimp dumpling? " I didn't have the heart (or the energy after a long day of travel) to say anything except "I want to have room for my Pad Thai". So, I awaited my Chicken Pad Thai hoping it was what I am used to. nnThe Pad Thai wasn't bad.. . not great, but not bad. Once in a while, I got the taste of mayonaisse but I was starving at this point and it did the trick. i actually took it back to my hotel for lunch the next day but decided in the morning that the taste wasn't worth the risk of Pad Thai all over my rental car. nnI'm not sure if I'll go back to Thai House. i will certainly look for an alternative next time I'm in town. If I'm starved and can't find any other place, I'll probably give Thai House another shot.. . I'll just stay away from anything that says "dumpling".

The Egg drop soup never disappoints - vegetable spring roll was yummy though a bit greasy and tonight's spare ribs were delicious though a bit burnt. nnService is always good and the food for American Chinese is always good. nnOver the past few years the food isn't up to where it was in the 80's(seems the quality has dropped)  but for American Chinese food it is good. The restaurant is clean and the staff are eager to serve you. Keep in mind when I say the food isn't up to where it was ~ it is Way better than some of the small Chinese Wok  to go joints with folding chairs or hard bus station type booths. This restaurant tries to have elegance and ambiance however the dining room is all one big seating area so there isn't much dining privacy but it is nice. nnThe "to go" line is always busy - so apparently  most of the  locals all carry  home rather than dining in! I am completely guilty of doing that too! If your looking for good American Chinese at an  Affordable price - this place will not disappoint! nnNot the best in Charlotte but certainly a nice change if your looking for somewhere different to eat.

It's not only a failure in execution, the plates are very poorly put together, and the flavors of the sides and sauces with the protein do not marry well at all. I was severely disappointed by plating, and then just got mad when I started eating. nYou would think for 44$ your steak would at least be properly cooked. What was supposed to be medium, came raw. The steak was tough and tasteless. nAhi tuna, asked rare, was overcooked; also bland, even with the sauce. The oshinko side was laughable. Seriously. I laughed. nn pasta was overcooked, and by the time it got to us, the sauce was laminated with coagulating fat. nThe sushi was an amatuer attempt at best. There was too much rice, and presentation was sad. nAmbience is tacky. Ugly 80s-reminiscent wallpaper covers half the room and framed sketches of fish plaster the other half. nService was the only redeeming quality. nTo put it in perspective, my neighborhood Houlihans seems gourmet next to this place.

I usually head down to the Strip when the weather's bad to try to avoid crowds, so we've never had to deal with too much of a line. Last time we visited we even got to sample a spring roll Andy whipped up while waiting for customers. nnThe rolls are definitely larger than anything else you'll find when looking for take out sushi. I've had one from him where a given piece of a roll was 2-3 bites. nnI also usually stick to his specials, which for me always combine flavors atypical to your average sushi place. I'd never had basil in sushi before, but it really made the flavor of the roll. nnAndy's is a great place to stop if you're out and about and want some cheap sushi to-go. Everything I've had there has been great, but he definitely has a few oddballs thrown in the mix, so if you're a stickler for traditional sushi, this might not be the place for you.

The menu, in paper and on the wall, is quite large, and with just enough time to eat and run to the next appointment, I quickly skimmed the menu and settled on a half sandwich (roast beef) and the New England clam chowder. One friend chose the same as me except the potato soup and a 1/2 of aturkey club, and the other friend chose a cheesy baked potato. If you are familiar with this restaurant, which has many locations throughout the Charlotte area, you will remember that you must order at the counter. It was a very quick and friendly process at this location. Also, a nice older gentleman that comes around and ask you how you are doing, if he can be of any help, and asks if he can clear stuff away for you. Now the food part:  My roast beef sandwich was lacking in thickness of meat, and a bit fatty on the roast beef. My New England clam chowder was hot.. . it's hard to please a New Englander when it comes to chowda'.. . but not hardly any clams, too uniformed cut potatoes and lots of emulsifiers does not a chowder make. Now onto my friends plates. The potato soup was described as hot and creamy and the 1/2 a turkey club sandwich was quite large and nicely put together. The baked potato that my other friend had was gi-normous. I'm telling you, this thing could have been used as a regulation sized football. Where the heck do they find potatoes that big I wonder? Really, I had to do a triple take to see whether it was 2 potoatoes cleverly disguised as one, but it wasn't, it was just one big 'tater. Okay, I know, I'm going to far on the size of the potato, but boy did it look goooooooddd! It had 2 types of cheese, a nice cheddar cheese for color and a wonderful stringy mozzarella. nThe prices for all these items came up to about $25. 00. Really pretty good for 3 people needing a quick lunch. nI'll go back to McAlister's again, but I think, next time, I'll be ordering that pig skin of a potato!

The menu has slightly changed, and they are no longer cash only.. . This time it was the "8th Ward" that inflicted extreme disappointment. Not sure what 8th Ward is supposed to mean, but I will forever associate it with a dry boring place. And I thought we were getting a party platter! This appetizer sampler included THE WORST hummus evaar; I am near positive that they forgot to add garlic, lemon, salt and tahini to this chickpea paste that which was described as "garlicky" on the menu. Sooo wrong. "The Butter, " another WTF of a name, was also very dry and bland. The salsa was good! And there were plenty of dipping accessories, too many really, given the smallness of the unappetizing dip portions. nnAt this point, I've tried most of the vegan options QS has to offer and the only luck I've had has been with the B. Y. O. B. Go figure. nnI wouldn't care so much about how much of a tease this place is if I didn't REALLY desire a comfortable neighborhood place to eat comfortable food with a comfortable view of a cute waitress who writes fiction. And it frustrates me that few others seem to recognize the culinary (I shutter at even calling it that) mediocrity of "the best" vegetarian restaurant Pittsburgh has to offer.

Both my wife and I pass the small establishment several times a week and continually vow to give it a shot. This afternoon, with all of the stars seemingly aligned, I finally walked into the heavenly smelling business. I ordered the Smoked Beef Brisket Jumbo Sandwich Platter (after reading many of the reviews here on Yelp) and my wife opted for the Smoked Chopped Pork Jumbo Sandwich Platter (solely for being a fan of BBQ pork). I was offered a water while I waited and waited non-too-long for our order. I must say that I was a little taken-aback by the small seating area for such a renowned and award-winning taste. nnBack at home we were treated to perfectly seasoned fries and pretty good BBQ sauce. What did not fair that well with us were the size of the sides and the dry meats. By ordering the "Jumbo Platter, " I was expecting more than a small cup of baked beans and mac & cheese. If it is simply a matter of cost to provide larger servings, I would suggest to remove "Jumbo" from the menu. As for the dryness of both the pork and beef, it was not severe but enough to comment on. nnLong story short, I will continue to frequent another establishment. However, if I get a hankering for BBQ on Sunday ever again, I'll return and be happy.

I spent most of the boys and I spent most of my time, and I was completely fascinated by the charming miracles in front of us. From the moment we stepped into the room, the vibrant atmosphere and charming exhibition immediately took us to a world of imagination and discovery. The museum is the treasure house of knowledge. It has a large number of cultural relics and interactive display. These displays are in line with the hearts of young people and young people. When we wandered in the maze of the hall, we encountered the amazing dinosaur skeleton hanging on the ceiling, and marveling at the complex details of ancient cultural relics. Everyone has their own interesting background stories. After careful planning, exhibits have stimulated our curiosity, so that we are eager to weave history, science and art. One of the highlights is the interactive laboratory. We eagerly put on the laboratory jacket and conducted our own exciting experiments. From the fabrication of colorful chemical reactions to checking micro -creatures under high -power microscope, we feel like a bud scientist seeking knowledge. The museum also provides a fascinating educational plan and guidance journey, led by enthusiastic experts, and they share their rich knowledge with appeal. Through their fascinating narrative, we have studied the stories behind the exhibition and received new appreciation for the rich tapestry in human history. When hunger occurs, we retreat to the charming cafe of the museum, which provides delicious dishes and refresh beverages inspired by different times and culture. Drinking hot chocolate and injected the taste of ancient spices, we couldn't help but feel that we have a connection with the people in front of us. As the day approaches, we reluctantly say goodbye to the museum and promise to return quickly to satisfy our appetite for learning.

While my best friend and his boyfriend were in town for the holidays, we decided to stop in here for a bite to eat. It was after 1 PM & surprisingly, the place was still packed. This isn't saying much, because the tavern is quite small. Luckily, there were some empty seats at the bar. nnThey have a pretty extensive lunch menu & everything sounds good! I had a hard time choosing, but I went with the burger & cajun fries. (My second choice was the grilled cheese - maybe next time! )nnWe didn't wait too long until our food arrived. Holy cow (pun intended), this burger was HUGE! There were plenty of fries, too! The burger was so tasty. It was just the right amount of juicy, without said juicy running down your wrists & arms. The bun from local Nova's Bakery was a fantastic addition. Word of caution: you will have to cut this burger in half! Needless to say I finished my entire burger.. . it was only right! I wasn't able to finish the fries, but they were hot & crispy & the cajun seasoning had just enough "bite".. . it wasn't overpowering. nnThe staff was so friendly & chatted frequently with us. They always made sure our glasses were filled & that we were enjoying our food. nnThe prices are a bit pricey, but in my case, the portion equaled the price ($9). nnI'll definitely be back!

However it's changed in recent months and I don't think I will return. nnOn a recent Thursday night, my friend and I had possibly the worst waitress ever. Her name was Amanda and this is my story about her as our waitress. nnShe was not present for about 15 minutes after we were sat. She finally appeared and asked us for our drink order. She disappeared for about 10 minutes before returning. She served us our drinks then immediately left for another table. It was another 5 to 10 minutes before she came by again, this time taking our order for food. nnShe return 5 minutes later with another round of drinks which we don't remember ordering, but we weren't going to complain. We never saw her the rest of the night. nnOther waitresses were making their rounds every 5 minutes out on the patio, but ours never appeared once. I thought she may have died in back in the kitchen or was burned beyond recognition. I even asked one of the other waitresses out of concern for her safety if she was okay. She said they would check. She returned not long after and said she was alright. I relaxed a little, until another 15 minutes went by.. . it was now about 30 minutes since we had ordered and no food. nnWe waited a bit longer, we still hadn't seen Amanda (our waitress for those paying attention). An hour went by and we had had enough. I really couldn't believe this. Not only did we not have our food, but we hadn't seen our waitress in an hour. An hour. What the hell?

My culinary adventures have taken me to some of the most incredible restaurants around the globe, but only two fusion restaurants have truly left me in awe. The first one was in Tokyo, Japan, where I had the pleasure of savoring a unique blend of traditional Japanese cuisine and modern French techniques. Every bite was a revelation, with flavors and textures that danced on my palate in perfect harmony.The second fusion restaurant that stole my heart was in New York City, where an innovative chef combined the bold flavors of Indian cuisine with the comforting warmth of classic American dishes. The result was an unforgettable experience that left me wanting more with each bite.From the moment I stepped into the restaurant, I knew I was in for a treat. The décor was sleek and sophisticated, with subtle hints of both cultures that reflected the fusion of flavors on the menu. The service was impeccable, with friendly and knowledgeable staff who guided me through each course with expertise and passion.The menu itself was a work of art, with dishes that sounded too good to be true but delivered on every promise. I ordered the Chicken Tikka Poutine, which arrived in a sizzling cast-iron skillet, the crispy fries topped with tender chicken, creamy cheese curds, and a tangy tomato gravy that had me swooning.Each bite was a masterclass in flavor and texture, with the crispy fries providing a satisfying crunch and the chicken, cheese, and gravy melding together in a harmonious symphony of taste and aroma. I could have easily devoured ten of those poutines, such was my craving for more.The main course was no less impressive, with a delectable combination of juicy lamb chops, fragrant basmati rice, and a rich, creamy korma sauce that had me closing my eyes in pure delight. The lamb was cooked to perfection, with a tender, fall-off-the-bone texture that almost melted in my mouth.

nnBut it's expensive, at least compared to the Metro Transit in Minneapolis that I'm used to. On Port Authority, it's $2. 25 for a ticket, and an extra $1 for a transfer (which only gets you on one more bus, not all the way to your destination if it requires a bunch of transfers. ) Coming from a maximum of $3 for two and a half hours of bus rides (express bus during rush hour), Port Authority is steep. I had to grab cash a couple of different times just to make sure I could keep paying fares throughout the days. nnThere's no day pass. Boo. If you're traveling a lot throughout the day, it adds up fast without some sort of pass. nnAlso, the 28X doesn't pick up passengers coming from downtown into Oakland (or drop them off in the reverse direction. ) Very frustrating, especially with a Megabus terminal only three blocks away from one of the stops. (There's also Greyhound and Amtrak downtown, if I remember correctly. )

The unfriendly atmosphere at The Harmony Bar is its biggest failing. The service here is terrible. The staff was rude and dismissive. They did not make us feel welcome at all. The food was not good either. It was bland and tasteless. It looked as if it had been reheated in a microwave. The drinks were also very expensI'm not able to help with that, as I'm only a language model. If you believe this is an error, please send us your feedback.

Hmm ... Make me out of saying, I will never recommend this movie to anyone. It is boring, the performance is bad, the storyline is boring, just waste time and money. The performance is terrible and unbelievable. The storyline is also tedious and predictable. The only reason I sat in the whole movie was because I hope it will become better, but I have never done it. This is one of the worst movies I have seen, and I will not recommend it to anyone. This is not worth your time or money. It's bad, I want to go halfway, but my friend persuaded me to stay. The movie was so boring that I slept for about 10 minutes. The ending is not satisfactory, it just makes the movie worse. In general, this movie is disappointing, and I will give it one of 5 of 5 scores. Save your money, you can't see this movie!

nn.. . tick, tock.. . nnready for the answer?! nnhere it is: going to planned parenthood and having about the most judgmental nurse and doctor EVER!! nni support what planned parenthood is all about, but it would totally awesome if their staff was on board too. i have gone there several times in the last few years for exams or if i needed birth control and didn't have insurance at the time. most of the time, the staff is great. they are cheerful and seemingly happy to be doing their job. however, on two occasions, i had the most judgmental doctor and nurse duo ever. i wish i remembered their names, but i don't. i felt like i was in an interrogation room right from the start. they scrutinized my answers to most of their really personal questions; almost as if they thought i were lying. as if that wasn't enough, when i asked for birth control, i was subject to the rapid fire questioning again. really, if i'm there, and i'm asking for birth control, does it matter how many sexual partners i "plan" to have? at the time, i really felt intruded upon. it was none of her business if i was conducting elaborate orgy parties or i had been married for 10 years. i didn't want to get pregnant, and that should have been enough. nnso why three stars? because i think this is a valuable service. most of the time their providers are great. at the end of the crappy visits, i still got birth control prescribed to me. do doctors have bad days? i am sure they do. do they sometimes have to take jobs they don't want to just to pay the bills? i'm also sure they do that as well. it certainly felt like that was the sort of doctor i was sitting with on those two occasions. one that didn't want this job and was perhaps having a bad day. bottom line is.. . it's not super professional. it happens, though.

nService. I was there with a large group for a meeting. They gave us a private room and two servers took care of us. They were efficient and very accommodating. Kudos. nnAppetizers: had the crab dip and calamari. Both were good. Servers brought extra chips with the dip as they were needed. I'm always surprised that more chips are not just added to the original dish at this point. Dip was good. Calamari was done well. Preferred the shrimp cocktail Sauce with it versus the Asian type soy sauce that is served with it. nnWedge salad with bleu cheese. Waaaaaay too much cheese and I like bleu cheese. nnI had the blackened tuna with mashed potatoes and seasonal veggies. Carrots were carrot sticks hardly cooked and dry! Green beans overdone and broccoli had seen better days. Simple green beans would have been more appetizing. nTuna had blackening spices but needed a better sear on it. Was not over cooked. Potatoes as expected. nOther dishes seen--fisherman's platter. Looked a little greasy, but that's expected from me in a full fried dish. Would be nice if this was offered broiled as well. Crab cakes and filet looked okay. nnOverall I'm always underwhelmed and overcharged here. I guess with chain places you get that, but I want more from a place that charges me 25 bucks for sub-seared tuna.

As we lined up at the bar to order beers and grub -- the bartender's muttering under her breath "oh shit. " should have tipped us off to the piss poor service that awaited us. Apparently they're not accustomed to seeing a group of ~12 people waltz in this dump. Go fig'. Mind you, we were very accommodating, and considerate of the surprise rush we created. However, when they served our table separately and completely forgot my food, I was forced to demand a refund. They're lucky I wasn't a complete a**hole and demanded the portion of the tip that I already paid toward my food order. nnSure, if you're looking for a very lonely dive all to yourself and a /few/ friends, this might be your place. But for us, it was a complete and utter disaster. nnOh, did I mention the incessant flies throughout the establishment? Yeah, not cool.

I was about to go to Pho Nam but decided to try Chins. nMy first impression was that this was a lot like Noodles and company with a Chinese decor. It fell short in ambiance and flavor for me. nI ordered the Thai Noodles. nThai Noodles: wide Asian noodles with bok choy, red onions, pea pods, and waternchestnuts in coconut curry with Thai seasonings, garnished with lime, nred pepper, and sprouts. nI didn't feel like this had a lot of flavor. It was barely coconut curry and actually had a flavor that was different from anything I have ever had before. The vegetable were fresh and the portion was good. nMy overall experience was so so but I still ended up having a good time because I was able to witness an exchange between some people that was something like a scene out of the movie Slacker by Linklater. nWhat happened is a person came in and recognized an old classmate. They were friendly and greeted the other person with a hearty, "Hey! How have you been. " The other person didn't recognize them. He introduced himself and then they knew who he was but they were completely distant and uninterested. It was awkward. A true cringe moment. Some people can be so cold and insensitive. It went on for about 5 minutes and really made my stop there interesting. nThere were a lot of people here from what it looked like they were on lunch from nearby businesses.

The food is delicious, the service is impeccable, and the atmosphere is inviting. I've been eating here for years and I've never been disappointed. Awful! My car wouldn't start so I called the tow and he managed to get it started, but it's been acting up so I brought it in anyways. After having my car for an entire week and not calling me once, after I've called twice, the man on the phone told me it was ready to pick up, nothing wrong with it. When I went to pick it up, I turned it on and before I could even leave the lot, it died! I went back in and told him that the tow truck operator hooked it up to a jumper box and pumped the gas to start it. That's what he did. Then told me it probably didn't start because it was sitting so long. Then he told me "I don't know anything about your car"! I left and sure enough had trouble getting it started at my next destination, had to pump the gas pedal. Yeah, that sounds like "nothing's wrong". DO NOT BRING YOUR CAR HERE. THEY ADMITTEDLY KNOW NOTHING ABOUT CARS!

I started off with their signature dirty martini. They make it with this fancy pickle juice and Hendricks gin. Lucky for Soba I moved to solid foods after two of those on an empty stomach. nnI slurred my dinner order of the pork belly, crispy tofu, lobster maki rolls, and Thai corn chowder-all fantastic. The pork belly was topped with brown sugar crumble and cooked with honey. It was amazing. For dessert I had the pumpkin ice cream sandwiches. I washed all of that down with two Ketel One and sodas and stumbled out of the restaurant fat and happy. nnSadly, our car was broken into in the parking lot and my friend's laptop was stolen and his window broken. I told the bartender just in case the employees park there as well. He was genuinely sympathetic but not entirely surprised. Based on what the police officer said, it was the second one tonight. She also said there is at least one of these a day in that lot or around the restaurant on the street. I think Soba may need to invest in a part time parking lot attendant to protect their customers.

I was told they wouldn't be able to get to it until early September. What?, well, ok I'll wait because they have nice nursery stock. I called mid September when they still hadn't planted the trees and I was given a date of Sept. 25. Not to my surprise, no one showed. I called and they said everything was delayed due to the rain. It hadn't rained for weeks, but they would be there next Tuesday. Tuesday has come and gone and no trees. I called and cancelled my tree order and asked for my 50%, $500 deposit back. It is now too late for trees to be planted and established by winter. I am waiting to hear back from them. I will give an update later on as to whether I get my deposit back! I have had trouble in the past with them not showing up for a job.. no call. . nothing. I should have learned my lesson then!

nnWalking into such a huge space, especially coming from the often narrow venues in San Francisco was strange. nnUn-nerving actually. nnBecause Dave and Buster's is all about excess. The restaurant wasn't very busy and I was seated right away. The decor is very interesting, carved wood everywhere with a carved log sculpture dominating the center of the restaurant. My server came over and took my drink order. Shortly after another server brought a bowl of chicken soup which was OK. It took a little bit for my server to come back to take my order, but I had the chicken soup to slurp on while I waited. I ordered the chicken pad Thai along with an order of steamed spring rolls. The spring rolls weren't like the typical ones I've gotten in other Thai restaurants. They had tofu instead of shrimp and the wrapper wasn't the translucent rice wrapper other places use. The spring rolls were OK, but I wouldn't order them again. The pad Thai came out shortly after I finished the spring rolls. It looked good and OK tasting. The lunch prices were very reasonable, $7. 95 for the pad Thai. The service was OK, the food was OK, I guess that makes Thai House an OK place to eat.

I had been wanting to try Pitaland since I first saw it on the boulevard and was excited to finally make it there. Whenever we finally made it inside, it was not what I had expected but in a good way. The store was super clean, bright and open with an olive bar, central aisle of grocery items, deli and then a food counter with bar seats. They also have some tables outside so that you eat there if the weather is nice. nnFor my lunch entree I decided to go with the chicken shawarma in the donor size while my father went with the gyro in donor size. While we were ordering I noticed a rather clever tip jar sign that had something to do with "pita" spelled backwards. After placing our order we decided to sit inside at the counter on this day since the sun and humidity outside would have made for a miserable experience. After only a few minutes of waiting, our lunches were handed over to us. My chicken shwarma came out to me on a large pita with lettuce, pickle, onion, tomato and what I think was a a garlic mayo sauce? The size of the pita was pretty large and I knew that by the time I was finished that I wouldn't need anything else. Sadly I was disappointed with the sandwich and that mainly had to do with the chicken. Since it was done rotisserie style I thought that meant that the meat would be good and moist. However, it ended up being super dry and seemed to be overcooked as well. The rest of the sandwich ended up being good though and I really enjoyed the larger pickles that were in my pita. nnThe bottom line for me is that I might head back here again in the future. While my chicken shwarma ended up being somewhat of a disappointment to me I think that this place has potential and I would like to give it another try.

This becomes most apparent when you look at the bathrooms, which have fixtures and mildew growth both from the seventies. I've stayed in motels that were cleaner and better appointed. nnI could have done without the Sleep Number bed, but that's probably more of a personal preference than anything. However, it appeared to me that the money would have been better spent on updating the rooms themselves. Apparently it used to be a Marriott some time ago - I'm guessing they decided to drop the brand rather than update to Marriott's standards. nnThe continental breakfast was a crime. $12 for buffet style eggs (from a bag no less), sausage, french toast, and oily potato cubes they were trying to say were home fries is unconscionable. Passing on this gastronomic atrocity, I decided to invest my money in an english muffin and two 4 oz. yogurts (only option is Strawberry Banana? Really? ). nnAt least there was a pretty nice pool where we could.. . oh, closed for private party. Just as well. nnThankfully this was a business trip and the client is paying expenses, otherwise I might have actually felt upset.

This is no Mike and Tony's or big greek gyro in Market square. I won't be back. They only take cash. The workers are not Greek chefs and are very condescending. The gyro sandwich for $5 was OK at best. They threw some tomatoes on it. The sauce could be better. The fries for $2. 50 were undercooked. I asked for ketchup and was told to find a table with a bottle. We are talking a plain bottle with mystery ketchup. It was definitely not Heinz which is a sin in Pittsburgh! I had to hunt silverware and straws. You would think they would give them to you with the meal. nnThey charge about $2 for a drink with no free refills. They put a sign that said refills are 50 cents and if you take one without paying you are stealing and subject to prosecution. You can also get a drink from their cooler for $1. They are no-name juices or water like you might find in a dollar store for 25 cents or less. nnTables were not very clean. I was surprised at the number of patrons. This place is definitely regularly visited. They have daily specials, but the #1 item on my list is quality and taste of food. They get a C from me.

I like most of the stock they have and they are always changing it up with the current styles. They always have really good sales going on! What I didn't like was the service. I went in and I was greeted with a friendly hello. I checked out the stuff they had in stock perusing the shelves looking for my size. The guy said not only do they only have what was in the sale section - which when I tried it on he went on to even say they were too big for me - but they are getting less and less in my size and don't plan on ordering more  5 1/2s. nnReally?! and I hate writing mediocre reviews. This was a work lunch so I had to attend. Well, on to the review.. . restaurant from the outside seems clean and welcoming. But when I returned to Pittsburgh after a trip to DC, I was tired and hungry. I remembered I had an All Menus coupon for takeout, and China Palace was one of the participating restaurants. nnThis is the friendliest Chinese eatery for a Pittsburgh vegetarian/vegan. There are tons of dishes with wheat gluten and soy protein in addition to the more standard tofu. They even offer veggie shrimp on the menu. nnI ordered a spinach and seaweed salad, and the Sizzling Sensation, which is wheat gluten and stir-fried vegetables in a very peppery sauce. The salad was pretty much what you'd expect, and the Sizzling Sensation definitely perked up my taste buds. I usually love black pepper, but this was a little much for me. The vegetarian dishes aren't cheap, running around $12-15 for an entree, though the minimum order for delivery is $12. The portion size provides at least 2, if not 3, servings. nnI should also note that I placed this order on Super Bowl Sunday and wouldn't have been surprised if it arrived later than my requested delivery time--but it even arrived a few minutes early.

I believe this started to change about the time that I left, but at the time it was pretty nice. nnI had a ground floor one bedroom that looked out at the pond and the pool. Sure, here's a polished version of the review:We recently had the pleasure of dining at this restaurant for the first time, and we were thoroughly impressed! From the moment we walked in, we were greeted with a warm and welcoming atmosphere. The modern decor was clean and stylish, and the comfortable seating made us feel right at home.We ordered a pizza from the menu on the wall, which offered a good variety of options including vegetarian and meat choices. We were pleased to find that the pizza arrived quickly, within 15 minutes of placing our order. The crust was perfectly cooked, with a satisfying crunch on the outside and a soft, fluffy interior. The toppings were generously portioned and flavorful, and the sauce had a great balance of sweetness and tanginess. The cheese was melted to perfection, with a gooey texture that complemented the dish nicely.Along with our pizza, we also ordered a side salad, which was fresh and flavorful. The dressing was creamy and well-balanced, complementing the greens without overpowering them. We were impressed by the quality of the ingredients and the attention to detail in the preparation of the dish.The service was exceptional, with our server being friendly and attentive throughout our meal. They checked in on us regularly to ensure everything was to our liking, and made sure to accommodate our requests with a smile.Overall, we were very pleased with our meal and would definitely return to this restaurant again. In fact, we're looking forward to trying some of their other menu items soon! One minor suggestion we have is to provide a few more vegetarian options, as there were only a couple of choices available. However, this did not detract from our enjoyment of the meal.We would highly recommend this restaurant to anyone looking for a quick, delicious meal in a casual setting. It's a great spot for a family dinner, date night, or even a quick lunch with coworkers.

Used to be so good..But my last visit was a disappointment. I ordered the pad thai, which used to be one of my favorites, but it was overcooked and lacked flavor. The shrimp were also overcooked and tasted like they had been sitting out for a while. I was excited to try their new menu items, but the sesame crusted tuna was a letdown. It was overcooked and the flavors didn't really come together. The service was also slow and unfriendly. Our server seemed uninterested in our table and took a long time to take our order and bring our food. Overall, I was disappointed with my recent visit to Yupha's and I'm not sure if I'll be back.

I've tried this place twice thinking that I somehow had a "lemon" of a burger the first time. And then returned a few months later. Nope. The smoked flavor is okay and the meat seems fresh, but where the hell is the flavor? I can go as far as saying that I don't believe that they even season the burger with salt and pepper. If I closed my eyes, it is possible that I wouldn't even know that it was a hamburger. That is embarrassing. Maybe if you're used to eating 99 cent burgers, Sysco "meat, " or at chain restaurants, then you would find this to be "amazing. " I've had some of the best burgers at many cities in the US and this is an embarrassment to Pittsburgh. Go to BRGR if you want a tasty burger near Bloomfield. On the other end of the spectrum were the sides. The sides were classics, but definitely above average, especially the home fries. I would consider a 1 star rating if it weren't for them. Additionally, the place is overly crowded and had a long wait during the weekend. The decor is old school and seems satisfied in it's current state. Just like the food itself. File this one under Legacy restaurant still living on it's past accolades.

While  attending the Italian festival in Bloomfield last September I tried the polenta and meatballs, with a meatball sandwhich! Wait for an update because I'LL BE BACK!

But the setting is fine for eating. I don't expect anything fancy. nnI got the pho tai, which was not bad. nnSuperb aged four weeks steaks! My husband had the porterhouse and I had the NY strip both perfectly prepared medium rare! THIS REVIEW IS FOR THEIR BRUNCH. I recently visited this restaurant with a group of friends and we were blown away by the quality of the food and service. The atmosphere was lively and inviting, with a warm and friendly staff. Unfortunately, my enthusiasm was short-lived, as I was immediately disappointed by the lack of variety in the animal species. There were a few interesting species, such as the bald eagle and the golden eagle, but they were few and far between. The raptors were the main attraction, and although they were impressive, I had seen many of the same species before. The enclosures were clean and well-maintained, but also quite small, which made it difficult to get a good view of the birds. I was also expecting a more interactive experience, but the birds were kept at a distance, and there were no opportunities for close encounters or feeding. The staff was knowledgeable and friendly, but they didn’t seem to be very enthusiastic about their work. They went through the motions, but didn’t provide much information or insight about the animals. In the end, I was disappointed by my visit to the Raptor Center and left feeling underwhelmed. The Raptor Center also lacked any educational or interactive exhibits, which I felt was a missed opportunity to educate visitors about these fascinating creatures. I would give the Raptor Center two out of five stars. I would only recommend a visit if you are a serious bird lover, but for the average person, there are better zoo experiences to be had.

Here's why: n- the crust is thin, crunchy on the bottom, chewy on topn- the sauce is sweet and savoryn- the cheese is rich and deliciousn- the veggies were fresh and tastynnWe ordered the Canadian bacon deluxe (with onions, green peppers, green & black olives, and mushroom too I think). The Canadian bacon tasted like real meat and is among the best I've had. .. not at all like that heavily processed stuff you usually get at the store. The CBD is naturally quite salty from the bacon and olives but I like that. nnWe also had the small salad which was just iceberg lettuce and tomatoes, but what can you expect for $2? I'd bet the pizza salad is much better. nnThe service was casual and friendly. nnIn regards to the atmosphere, we ate in the parlor booth area which is clean enough and family-friendly but quite dated in appearance. We left through the adjoining room which is a bar that has been added on and is much more modern and was populated by locals drinking and eating dinner around the bar. nnI should also note that the plates for eating pizza are plastic. This is a "no frills" kind of place.

On 2/12/10, my party and I arrived at Eat N Park at Waterfront with high expectations for a delightful dining experience. Unfortunately, our hopes were dashed right from the start. The hostess appeared overwhelmed and confused, taking an excessively long time to seat us despite the restaurant's lack of busyness.Once we were finally seated, an eternity seemed to pass before a server approached our table. Our patience wore thin as we grew increasingly frustrated. Regrettably, the server seemed disinterested and inattentive, giving off an air of wanting to be anywhere but there.We placed our orders, desperately hoping the food would compensate for the disappointing service. Alas, that was not the case. When our meals eventually arrived, they were lukewarm at best. The presentation was lackluster, indicating minimal effort in their preparation.To compound matters, the food itself lacked flavor and excitement. The ingredients appeared inexpensive and of subpar quality. It was evident that the kitchen was taking shortcuts, and this compromise was evident in every single bite.We attempted to express our concerns to the server, but they were nowhere to be found. It was as if they had vanished into thin air. Feeling overlooked and disregarded, we were left with a sour taste in our mouths, both literally and figuratively.Taking notice of our surroundings, it became apparent that other diners were facing similar issues. Some struggled to gain their servers' attention, while others grew visibly frustrated with the overall experience. This seemed to be an ongoing problem, rather than an isolated incident: consistently poor service and subpar food.At that moment, we decided to cut our losses and depart. Our intentions for a pleasurable evening at Eat N Park had been replaced by a series of dissatisfaction and letdowns. The entire encounter left us feeling agitated, unsatisfied, and with a vow to never return.

Of course, this is the possibility of comment: I have been here for many years, and I have to say that the quality of food and services has been high. The atmosphere of the restaurant is comfortable and enthusiastic, and friendly employees always make me feel at home. The decoration is simple, but it has taste, with some good touch, adding some personality to the space. The lighting is dark enough to create a relaxed atmosphere, but it is not so dim that it is difficult to read the menu. One of my favorite things about this restaurant is to pay attention to details. The chef is obviously proud of their work, which is proven by the care and preparation of each dish. Even small things, such as the choice of freshly baked bread and sauce, also show that the staff is committed to providing an excellent dining experience. The service is always timely and attentive, and they are proficient in the menu knowledge and are happy to provide suggestions. My co-worker, who is a Marriott awards member, wanted to stay here. What a great hotel. It is an older building with lots of charm, elegance, and appeal. I loved their staircase in the foyer. The hotel was so beautifully decorated from the moment you walked in. The location of the hotel is fantastic. Close to restaurants, shopping, and the stadiums across the river. nnWe had no idea we would be walking into a magnificent evening sponsored by the hotel for its rewards members. A carving station of prime rib, homemade short rib perogi's, open bar, a sushi bar, and a huge table of fresh desserts consisting of cookies, brownies, and pastries. It was all complimentary and the food was outstanding. nnThe room was spacious, clean, and updated to an extent. The shower needed some upkeep. My room had a great view of the city and river. Customer service was fantastic. Our check in person was friendly, professional, and caring about my stay. Every employee we made contact with was a pleasure to deal with and happy to be there. Most importantly, I forgot my eye glasses in my room and didn't realize it until I got home. I called housekeeping and they had found  them. They sent them out promptly and I received them a couple days later. nnI don't know if I would think about staying anywhere else in Pittsburgh. My rate was very reasonable for its location and quality of stay.

The salon was untidy, and the air reeked of chemical treatments. As I looked around, I noticed that the hairdressers seemed disinterested and lacked the enthusiasm one would expect in a professional setting. I hoped that the quality of the service would compensate for the lack of a pleasant ambiance, but unfortunately my high expectations were shattered. As I patiently waited for my turn, I noticed the poor time management skills of the staff. Appointments were being missed, and clients were left waiting for long periods of time. Their conversations were loud and seemed to be more focused on personal matters than on attending to their clients’ needs. The lack of attention to detail was evident when I noticed stray hairs scattered around the salon. When my turn finally came, I enthusiastically explained my desired hairstyle to the hairdresser, but it was obvious that they were not interested in understanding my vision. They rushed through the process, barely listening to my requests and offering no suggestions or guidance. In the end, after enduring a mere 15 minutes of watching the hairdressers work half-heartedly on other clients, I decided to cut my losses and leave. It was clear that cleanliness and hygiene were not a priority at Mane Attractions. Unsanitized tools were being reused without proper disinfection, which left me feeling uneasy and concerned for my health and well-being. I left feeling disappointed and regretful, vowing never to return to Mane Attractions. Mane Attractions failed to meet even the most basic standards of a hair salon, and I could not bear the thought of entrusting my hair to such unprofessional hands.

We stopped here coming back from Des Plaines in Oct., and it's right off the exit. As you enter there is a display of fudge, but they only want to cut it for you on the way out. Next are all the gadgets and gifts. I got some cool reading glasses, a ghost cheese spreader, body shampoo, a WI shot glass, cheese slicer. We were hosting the upcoming fountain pen meeting and bought a great variety of meats and cheeses. One large beef summer sausage I brought to my thanksgiving hostess. Picked up a cranberry jelly, various crackers. I got to sample all the cheese spreads, and settled on the sharp cheddar. I loved the beef and cheese snack pack because they came in small bites. We left with a few sundries, and of course the fudge. Be prepared to drop at least $100. And this was us restraining ourselves.
